text:
We checked in, or tried to, on Tuesday 26th Nov. We had flown up from Florida and had been travelling all day, so to be greeted by a long line of angry and frustrated fellow guests and having to join the queue, was not what we wanted or expected. We found out from other guests the computers were 'down'. There was no management to inform us of the problems, or pacify the
guests. The man who joined the queue behind us was about to explode with rage at the situation! During the hour we remained in the queue, a couple of junior members of staff came by and offered us a bottle of water. It was very cold outside and we suggested the guests may be happier receiving hot drinks, snacks and somewhere comfortable to sit. None was available. There are very few chairs in the beautiful lobby and nearly always occupied, as this is the only place with free wifi access. When we did get to check in, some coffee and tea appeared, but too late for us.
Finally, we got to our room but our key card would not let us gain entry. The porter called reception who thought it may be booked out to another guest, when this matter was cleared up, security then arrived to try to gain entry. All failed. My husband then went with security back down to reception. I stayed with our luggage and the porter outside the room. Some 45 minutes later we were eventually in our room. The whole process of having stepped foot in the hotel at 3.30pm and gaining entry to our room at 5.30pm took more time than our flight from Orlando to JFK!
We had booked a superior master double room and although it was a corner room with nice double aspect windows and a separate dressing room leading to double washbasin area and then bathroom, it was not superior in furnishings or décor. You couldn't even call it 'shabby chic', it was just plain shabby. The room needs redecorating top to bottom. Inside drawers were dirty and the toilet kept self flushing.
On Thanksgiving Day (28th) we had not been pre warned that the hotel's restau

Use this hotel as a last resort. Good location though. Rooms are noisy, beds are uncomfortable, parking is tough. No free breakfast. Took two men and a boy just to turn on the shower. The room smelled stale.

Wow! What a disappointment! We regularly stay here. It was our favorite hotel, but I used some of Hilton Honors points to stay for 2 nights for New Year's Eve expecting to have a great time. Instead, when we checked in we waited in a line almost 40 minutes, then we were given 1162...it reeked of mold. I called the front desk, but no one answered, so my husband went down to the lobby. After 15 minutes more, he found a manager. She said she would send an inspector up to the room to see what was wrong. We then had to wait for an inspector who was a young girl who came up to the room and asked me where I thought the mold was coming from?? At this point, we were late for our reservation, had no room to unpack and get ready for New Years Eve and had to get ready in the bathroom while we left our bags to be moved to another room. Ok, so we returned after 1 am and were re-assigned to 1115. There the heat was cranked so you could not even turn it off. I called the front desk but gave up when no one answered. The hotel left an apology note and 2 drink vouchers under our door. It gets better. We asked to have the room cleaned on Day 2 and housekeeping cranked the heat up when we had shut it off and put our pillows in front of the window we had pryed open like an inch. They left the used glasses dirty and where they were and did not give us any clean ones. We arrived back to find now that our keys did not work. I asked the bell captain and anyone else I could find if they could just make us another key, but was forced back into the check in line and had to waste almost another 30 mins waiting for new keys!!,,,,Btw, the only reason we went out was to get some cola and water for the room because the minibar was locked and we were never asked if we wanted a key, so we had to go to Duane Reade for Coke and water. Ok so when we checked out, we decided to use our drink vouchers for a coffee and a tea in Peacock Alley. We waited 15 mins to be seated in a lobby bar, then we were seated

First of all they are remodeling and the lobby is torn up and the halls are sweltering hot! The first night they put me in room 1308 which is up against the vending machines that hum loudly and vibrate the bed. I complained, but they said they did not have another smoking room available for me so I did not get to sleep until after 2am after driving 14 hours from Oregon and arriving there drop dead tired at 10pm! The next night they gave me a complimentary 'upgrade' to a suite room 1318 where the microwave didn't work and the bedroom air conditioner did not go high enough to cool off the room! They did give me a discount for the first night, but it did not make up for the nights sleep I lost. I was very disappointed since I stayed at this Ramada in May and had a delightful stay! I travel to California several times a year and since my first stay was enjoyable I had decided to make this hotel my 'home away from home' however, I don't know if I will stay there again! I will say that the staff was very nice and polite and they have a decent free breakfast!

All the food here gave me food poisoning period. There was urine in the toilet upon entering my first room and the manager offered me a gift certificate which didn't work.

The room is small and cramped and the views are mediocre. Mostly of construction.

Almost died spending the weekend here eating the overpriced food poisoned food.

Then when you complain about the food they want to send security up.

Pfft yeah right I'm never dealing with security.

2 stars for the location. That's it.

The "deluxe" room we stayed in over the weekend was both suffocatingly small, and the bathroom resembled what one might find in a $49 Motel 6 outside of Omaha. The paint in the bathtub was cracked in a half dozen places, and the cabinets were crooked (not by design). There was a vent that was caked with lint and it appeared as if it hadn't been cleaned in months if not years. For $800 a night, this is truly unacceptable and shocking.

You are much better off staying at a smaller property where the staff can actually take care of the rooms.

I stayed here for 3 nights. The hotel is OK, rooms are tiny, very very small even to manhattan standards, but the hotel was not bad, until I checked out.
I was charged $31 per night for the 'urban fee', something apparently was not included in the hotel rate. They said I signed for this when I checked in, but when they made me sign that form they did not say that was for a fee, they said it was just a signature they needed. Actually, the guy said 'no need to fill the whole form, we just need your signature'.
Apparently you pay $31 per day for internet, access to the fitness center and a welcome drink. But you cannot opt out of this, there is no way to say 'don't give me internet, but save me the $31'.
The reason they do this, is because they can advertise a lower rate, win over competitors in search engines, and then charge an additional $31 per night (20% of their rate) at checkout. So, my advice, do not compare this hotel to other hotels of $150, because the nightly rate is well above $180, and if you take that into account, there are better options.
Besides this, the sole fact that they do this is something not nice at all. I prefer to be treated well as a customer, and not to be scammed like this.

Having said all this, the rooms are OK and clean, the service is good, and the bars and restaurants at the hotel are also good. If it wasn't because of this bad business practice, i would have ranked them 4 or 5, but this is not the way guests should be treated.

Requested early check in as I was arriving around 8:00 am after a 20+ hr Int'l flight. Accepted reservation and then sent email they will try. Front Desk to the left as you enter lobby but no sign giving directions. Had to wait 90 mins for room. Snotty attitude by front desk clerk. Asked Bellman to store luggage and got upset till I reminded idiot room was not ready. Tiny room, tiny bathroom for a highway robbery price. Lousy room service meal. Phone antiquated system as you had to give room # to kitchen staff. Egg Omelet with mushroom was garbage - mushroom pcs thrown on top. Did use salt from shaker to gargle sore throat !!!!! Still using old style "privacy cards" on doors. More expensive than Jaipur (India) Marriott and worthless is every aspect. If Corporate Marriott thinks this is a jewel, somebody has been snorting something up their nose !!!!! Not worth wasting time or money. This is good for cheap and stingy airlines to use for their crew who just need a place to rest for few hrs. If I could give "0" stars, I would do that !!!!!

They constantly mocked me and was extremely rude . They quoted me one price and then charged me another . They are acrivtening group of miserable people who mock the guests . Absolutely horrible and frightening experience

I stayed in this motel for 2 night. You will smell thecooking smell from the apartment, parking is very tight, the owner Rick is very mean, I thinks both husband/ wife run the motel. You she the wife working in the garden showing her butt.. It was horrible stay, I think you can find verypalce by 10 doller extra to your bill. Area is very noisey...

Seriously horrible. This place has gone downhill big time. I stayed here about 3 years ago for a couple of weeks, that how nice it used to be. This time, I didn't make it much past check-in time. Plus the front desk staff were super snooty. STAY AWAY from this hotel!!

I've stayed at this hotel several times over the last decade. I've written positive reviews, but I have to downgrade the property based on the service levels experienced.

Here were some of the issues:
* I called the hotel a couple times before my stay to place some special requests. I experienced very poor service from the front line people taking my calls. This makes a bad first impression.
* The SPG platinum line was not staffed. There was an unusually long line given I was checking in at midnight after a long flight.
* I left my wallet in my room after visiting the restaurant for dinner. When I checked at he front desk, I was told security would have to let us into our room and check my ID when we got it. Ok, that seemed reasonable, but they left my wife and I standing in the hallway for 25 minutes waiting for security. And, after all of that, they just let us in without checking my ID.

Some of the good:
* I called for room cleaning during windows that we were out of the room. They were prompt and thorough.
* I did finally get through all of the gate keepers (who were rude) and spoke to a front desk manager who was very helpful with a special request.

One more big disappointment.

I look forward to the Westin Heavenly Bed. Unfortunately, the bed we had was old and broken down. I have back issues. The sagging bed made sleeping almost impossible and walking the next days painful.

This has been my "go to" hotel in LA for 15 years, I'm not so sure anymore.

Gold elite member, originally booked and "guaranteed" with Marriot Visa, when I arrived I was informed my requested room type was not available, even though I had a confirmation from earlier in the day, making me wonder what was confirmed. I was asked by the front desk agent Evelyn whether I had requested two queens vs one king, and I promptly showed my confirmation email and was not asked that question again,... frankly, I found that insulting, pretty sure the system showed it if used correctly. Zach Wilborn (Evelyn had no last name on her badge, making me think she was either new or junior) did attempt to save the evening by getting me into a Courtyard room with two queens, the room was satisfactory and the bed provided a good nights sleep. I did not use the breakfast because I slept in, however I found nothing special about this property other then frustration about no attempt make up the failure of having my confirmed room prepared. I may likely seek another solution next time, luckily here in downtown Los Angeles, there are many other options.

After our recent stay, it's likely to be our last at this hotel due to a series of issues with them charging our credit card and their management refusing to help us rectify the issues, not responding to our calls, and being disinterested in even helping us.

Several days after the deposit holds were removed from our credit card and settled, we noticed on our statement that there were two settled charges and we were double charged for our stay. We spoke to accounting and were told they would look into the double charges... several days later, we never heard anything.

Days later, we got a fraud notification from our bank saying the Hyatt House LAX was trying to charge an additional ~$1600 to our card. We indicated this was not authorized, so our bank closed our card. When we reached out to the Hyatt House to find out why this happened, the front desk agent was apologetic and said they could see the random charges, but were not sure why it happened. They said we'd hear back from management next day to resolve the issue.

After not hearing anything from management, I reached out several times but never got a call back. I ended up having to call Hyatt corporate to nag the accountant to get back to me. Their response? For the original double charge, just dispute it with my card company. For the $1600 in random charges? I was told, "we're sorry." No explanation why the fraudulent charges, who had access to make those charges. Their accountant did try offering 5000 Hyatt points eventually for the inconvenience and tried convincing me they're worth $5,000 (1 Hyatt point per $1) which is absolutely not true. When I questioned them on this and their accountant said they'd call me back, once again, I still have not received a response.

Nice place to stay if nothing goes wrong. If something happens, good luck trying to get any sort of help out of their lame excuse for management.

The staff was not friendly or engaging. The maid staff was unable to communicate, "No Habla Ingles", which is very frustrating when you’re only trying to obtain two bath towels. I don't expect the staff to be completely fluent, but they should at least know some basic words like "Towels"! Also, at 3AM on the first night of my two night stay, something - not sure what, ran up my arm while I was sleeping. I suspect it was a cockroach, but it could have been a fairly large spider. Not sure because it escaped my attempt to kill it. Anyway, didn't sleep much after that. Unfortunately, I had to stay the second night due to lack of availability in the area and I had business to conclude. My wife and I have stayed at Best Western Hotels around the world, but this establishment was totally lacking.

Horrible.
I'm not going to pretend to be a travel writer so I'll just list the facts and opinions:
-The entire hotel is filthy. The rooms have scuff marks all over the walls, the carpets are stained, the elevators are disgustingly dirty with broken floor tiles.
-Decor is depressing. Who chose these colors? Clearly not an interior design with any talent. The halls are dark with walls and carpeting in what my 10 year daughter aptly describes as "swamp green." The doors are scuffed. The carpet is stained. Room service trays lay about.
-Restfullness. The beds are cheap and motel-y feeling. The walls are PAPER THIN. You can hear everything in the neighbors rooms and the hallway. We were up all night with sounds coming from everywhere. Neither my wife nor I slept a wink (and we were not even facing the pool.)
-View. Our room was a "city view" room. What we saw out our window was a parking lot and drainage canal. Pretty sure I didn't see a city.
-Room service: The breakfast card menu is very limited so you cannot pre-order many of the items on the actual menu. No pancakes or waffles for the kids today. One thing I will say is that the room service was prompt.
-Front desk. Very mildly friendly.
-Value at $199 in LA. You can do much better, even if you have to be stuck in the Valley. (see Garland, Universal Sheraton, Hyatt Andaz or Sunset Towers on other side of hill, etc.)

I would never consider going back, Fortunately, it was only one night when we had to be out of our house a few miles away and was not a special occasion.

I have been to a couple banquettes there. Terrible food.

Why is it called the Sportsman's Lodge anyway? There is nothing sporting or lodge like about it. There are random quotes on the walls from people like Marilyn Monroe and Bruce Springsteen that have no connection to one another or anything obvious about the hotel.

Absolutely horrible hotel. Staff are rude and unhelpful which makes it frustrating to even ask a question. Asked the front desk to call to arrange a shuttle bus only to have them point to the computers and say it would take us to long and to do it ourselves on the internet. No wifi in the rooms unless you pay extra. For the sum of money I had to pay for the stay I feel absolutely ripped off. Asked by not one but two people if we were escorts as apparently they frequent the hotel for guests hmm Only good thing was the location

My wife and I decided to stay over after a cinespia screening this weekend. Looks beautiful when you get there from the lobby and throughout. Rooms were OK at first glance, we just dropped our stuff and took off after checkin. Seemed pretty quiet until the bar crowd got back at 2:30 and decided to continue their parties in our floor. Front desk didn't seem all that surprised and said they'd get security on it. Took two calls to at least get the music off, screaming continued. Mentioned it at checkout, they were pretty dismissive.

I've made this hotel my home away from home for the past 11 years. Unfortunately pandemic has been very bad to it. Its dirty, the walls are dirty and scuffed, the carpets need vacuumed, the smell of Marijuana overwhelms, and vaping. The first room they gave us was full of fruit flies. The bedding was stained and had holes. Cigarette burns on the toilet. The clientele has changed dramatically. There is a skeleton crew running it. Gone are the friendly faces year after year of the same bell boys, housekeepers, and valets. They have been replaced by young punk security guards at the door!!! So sad. The workers behind the front desk are excellent. The other downside is they have closed all entrances except the front lobby, no east side entry. I'm sad. I want the old biltmore back.

4 issues- first billing - I stayed 1 night hotel and when I checked My credit card was preauthorized for the standard $50 for incidentals (as room was paid in full in advance $343 for 1 night). That day I knew I would be charged the $28 for self parking Then the next morning (the day I was checking out) They (without telling me) ran a second preauthorization for $130.80, while the $50 was still on hold. I asked them about this and they said the computer decided since I had the $28 parking, it had to up the hold, but they didn't only up it, they reauthorized - so now I had $180.80 in preauthorizations for the day I was checking out (with only being a 1 night visit). When I checked out I was told my total charges were $80.94 for parking and the breakfast that morning. Well they then ran a new charge for the $80.94. It is now 5 days later and I was just lucky I had that much credit on my card as there is stall a total of $261.74 being held on my card for 1 prepaid night :( What the heck? I know you can final out the hold for the correct amount and not run a new charge, and why were they putting the second hold anyway, when at the time they ran the $130 I had no room charges except the $28 for parking and I was checking out in 2 hours.

2nd 3rd issues, The room looked very clean on the surface however, I got 14 bed bug bites all across my chest and the bed was horribly uncomfortable for a $343 a night room. NEVER AGAIN

4th-was not informed hotel was under major construction

Poor service for a well known brand. Staff not interested in assisting, just apologise and say out of our hands. Too many issues, toilet not working, small TV in room, no executive lounge, more geared toward conference facility than family friendly hotel.

This was mine and my families first say at a Hiton Hotel. Whilst the hotel and our room were comfortable and clean, the customer service needs some work.
Upon check in, we were 'greeted' by a lady at the desk who didn't say hello, and didn't appear to be interested in our reservation. My husband was asked if he was parking at the hotel, my husband confirmed we were not... clearly preoccupied, the lady asked the same question again.
Whilst updating our information into the system, the lady turned her back to us and started talking to a colleague. clearly uninterested in the job she was doing. When she had finished the discussion, she then came back to us. at this time, my husband confirmed we were Hilton Honours membors, the response we got was, "well I cannot see that here, and it's going to take some time to find this so I will check when you go to your room". We have not received any confirmation from the Hilton that we have stayed there on our Hilton Honours account and the lady did not at any time confirm that we would get credits onto our card.... we're still waiting!

Everything about this stay went wrong, and it was not the fault of the physical facilities, but that of the management and staff. Lost an important article, and their security shrugged. While checking out, desk clerk threw away my parking stub and then blamed me for him doing it. Considering that I am the highest level elite in their frequent guest program, one would think they would try a little harder. Never again--I'll stay at the Marriott next door and avoid this hotel's monumentally incompetent management. Upon check-in, they informed me that the hotel would not have water for at least four hours, and when I asked them why they didn't bother letting me know until after they swiped my card, they just stared at me blankly like they had no coherent, cogent or comprehensible response.

I stayed at this hotel a couple of times in 2012 and decided to give them the benefit of the doubt and once again stay in January 2013.

The shuttle service for this property is significantly flawed, which was an issue in my last review of the LAX Hilton as well. Of course, the hotel will give you the standard line, they do not operate the shuttle, which is accurate. However, they are responsible for any supplier to represent their product in the best light possible and the existing arrangements with their shuttle company is anything but, the best light. Be prepared to wait for an unacceptable time for the shuttle to the property which is only just over a mile down the road. Your frustrations will become exacerbated when you see competing hotels' vans come and go, multiple times. This includes vans from the Marriott LAX, Holiday Inn LAX, amongst others. Very, very frustrating.

Upon the long awaited arrival to check-in, I was greeted by lines and an understaffed front desk. Even the HHonors line had five individuals and one, yes one, front desk person. I arrived prior to the HHonors Executive check-in within the Executive lounge on the 16th floor, so there was no alternative but to wait in the line. While I found the front desk agent to be professional in appearance, I would not classify this individual to be well trained, and in fact, should not have been handling the likely most experienced travelers within the HHonors line. Amenities at the hotel were never discussed, even the most elementary welcome to me as a HHonors Diamond member, never occurred. If I was the management at the hotel, it would be an embarrassment.

The room was purely average and was a disappointment. Again, no recognition for my HHonors level of membership, nor was there any sort of letter, memo, amenity welcoming me to the property. Inexcusable in my opinion.

I had breakfast in the restaurant and apparently the buffet line was "under construction" and therefore the buffet was set up on banq

One of my least favorite Hilton hotels. The hotel was old and shabby (even though there is restoration going on). The shower in my room was weak and the water was not hot. The reception staff were terrible (slow and highly unhygienic). The elevators worked only 50% of the time (out of 6, 2 worked, 1 was stuck and the others never arrived). The service at the bar was ghastly, food inedible, and rude waiters.

My Visit to Los Angeles was ruined as I returned to my room this evening, the second of a three day Christmas visit home to be with Family. I went to the restroom in my room to find that someone left me a gift in the Toilet!!! It was not mine!!! Someone, I am sure was Housekeeping had used the Toilet and closes the lid and did not Flush!!! How RUDE!!! First, to use a Guest restroom!! I understand when the need is urgent!! I understand not wanting to take a break so you can go home early on Christmas Day. I am grateful that someone was working today. But clearly YOU forgot to FLUSH!!! Second, it also brings to mind that they also forgot to clean the Toilet after they used it, which again makes you wonder if they actually cleaned the room. They forgot to FLUSH. Management was rude and not very responsive. I had to go to the Front Desk twice. The Manager went so far as to suggest that " someone" had made a duplicate key to use the toilet!!!
REALLY!!!!! To use the toilet!!! I am done with the Hilton LAX!!!

i always liked the hilton in los angeles, i stayed a twice before in this one but now it really has declined in everything except in prices ...
i seldomly have encountered a more unpersonal and ugly 'luxury' hotel than this. we stayed right now for 3 days and were astonished how this place has developed into a hotel factory.
not the slightest detail of courtesy,barbaric ,yelling hordes all over the place, door banging neanderthals from asia and europe, not sufficiently clean rooms for a hilton,breakfast basic and no one eventually gives a ..... how that might appeal to discerning tourists. i tell you how,we will never ever come back to this pitiful hilton.
250 us fr this ???
almost no soundproof windows,so you can be aware of all landings the whole afternoon.
noisy,dated and too expensive !
the entrance is like a walmart, the guests are like let loose or all with drugs, i saw three LAPD officers dragging out a drunk alcoholic in handcuffs of the elevator,almost naked and entirely covered by tattoos. where do they find those lowlife delinquent guests???
internet only free in the lobby,in the room they rob,sorry charge you like 20 bucks,good joke.
the only positive thing is the free and pretty frequent shuttle to lax.

then at 9 in the morning on sunday they have to vacuum the hallways with an abominable noise? what kind of place is that? a hilton? gettoutahee !
never ever again this place...
even the sheraton in miami (have a look at my review) was better than this hole.
shame on you hilton lax !!!

Wow. Shocked at the rave reviews.

I'm a Spire Member and was "upgraded" to a "newly renovated" room. I'd hate to see the non-renovated rooms. As you'll see from the attached photos, there were some serious maintenance issues in our room, which was also just generally grotty (quoting from another reviewer).

Check in was good, but that was our only positive experience. No one helped with suitcases, though there were three idle hotel employees waiting curbside. The gym and pool were both uninviting. And though we were invited to the 16th floor lounge for breakfast, we skipped it - assuming that it too was going to be similarly soul-crushing.

I get it. Airport hotels aren't known for quality. You sleep there and leave. That's it. With that said, I was hoping for more out of this IHG experience.

I've stayed at this hotel before & the location was perfect along with the hotel being very nice itself, so I thought I'd try it again. Well not only was the same construction going on at the freeway exit to get to the hotel but there are no detours and no instructions from the hotel as to how to get there, it took me no less than 1 hour to get back to the hotel after leaving for dinner. The insane parking fee was supposed to cover safe parking at night but the valets parked other cars so close to me that I couldn't open my doors in the am when I needed to leave. The room was fine since I paid more for a suite, but I had the privacy sign on the door and the maid STILL knocked on my door! When it was time to leave and battle LA traffic, I called the bell desk, called the valet, called the front desk-numerous times and to no avail-not 1 person helped me to my car with my luggage, and when I was in front of the valets trying to load up my car, they didn't so much as offer a hand....seriously BAD service here. To top it all off, they are charging my card for mysterious charges that I did NOT incur. On the phone with them now and they are VERY unhelpful-HORRIBLE SERVICE! Stay somewhere else

Wife and I booked this hotel in connection with the "American Beauty Show" being held at McCormick Place. We received a good rate at $99 and it appeared to be located in a very convenient location to other activities in the city. My wife has a very serious food allergy and requires an in-room micro and fridge to prepare all meals. When I called to book this room I was connected to reservations and was told this room would have these amenities (leading me to believe that all rooms there had the same as a standard item). Since this was such a necessary item for our stay I called a second time, a day before the reservation, to confirm and once again was told there would definitely be a micro/fridge located in our room. (No need for a specialrequest) After driving four hours to arrive, we checked in and discovered no micro / no fridge. We called the desk and was told there were none available (even at a charge). We were offered an option to possibly work out a solution the following day ...but this was no help as we had to prepare and store meals beginning that evening. In their defense the offered to store her food in a fridge at the desk but her allergy is so severe that it is risky not to isolate her items. Initially they had mentioned a suite option (which had micro/fridge) but was told this would come at the increased price. I told them I would accept this solution but after checking was told it would not be available for my length of stay.
After an hour conversation with someone from Hampton Inn guest services (1-800) we were left with no alternative but to check out and return home. There were no other alternatives offered by the Hampton Inn personnel. We had to make the four hour return home that night, missing the 3 day conference (pre-paid). My wife also lost pay for the 3 day conference which she was to receive. Guess that $99 special rate at Hampton Inn was not so good afer all. My main complaint here is that first of all ..when booking or checking a reserva

Our room was a disaster - the toilet and bathroom sink would not drain, the wall had multiple cracks, water damage, etc. the floor/carpet was dirty- the reception was typical - for the price - multiple better places to stay

I was recommended by a friend to stay here and after my stay I will no longer stay there or speak to that friend. Hotel staff very over the top and pushy, asked for early check in (I know it is hard to get those) but the staff was very rude about it and promised a text when the room was ready and did not text me, they said "we forgot we were busy." It is a standard room and nothing too fancy about the room for almost 1000 a night. My wake up call was not placed so I was late to a meeting I had. Room service took way too long for the meal I ordered. Just overall a disappointment and I will not stay there anymore. The manager at the front desk, the tall skinny woman with the blonde hair, phony compassion and not helpful at all.

I have stayed here numerous times this past year for business. Typically it is quite pleasant, as per my other review from January. My company always books an executive room with a view (preferably of the river) in the Executive Tower. My April stay, however, was not at all up to par. The check in was completed by an employee with a very poor attitude. My reservation was for an executive room in the Executive Tower (as it is always booked). When I arrived, the very non-service-oriented female working the Honors desk told me that she needed to "balance the house". What this meant is that they were unwilling to honor my Executive Room reservation in the Executive Tower and instead placed me in a "Classic Room" with no view in the not-at-all "Grand Tower". I should elaborate - the front desk female employee tried to sell this room as "very nice with a great view of Michigan Ave." I know I have high standards, but I do not consider a low level floor view of the Under Armour store to be a nice view.... The female manager that evening and the next day refused to make any attempt to move me to a better room. I spent my entire stay in a cramped, dingy room with no view and two twin beds. Most disappointing!!!!! Terrible customer service and a very lame "balance the house" excuse by a female front desk employee and a female front desk manager. I'll be sure to stay elsewhere on my next trips this year - so many great choices in Chicago!

I recently made a TripAdvisor Review about this hotel on April 18th. In light of what I found out about my hotel charges and my ignored email, I have decided to delete and update this review to reflect a more accurate perception of the hotel. Please refer to my two new logged-in paragraphs labeled “UPDATE”, especially for the hotel’s social media/ guest services manager Mr. Andrew. B and the Front Office. My overall findings of my stay were conflicted to the point I’m relatively upset by this.

Last Tuesday (April 12th) was a very special day for me, which was my 21st Birthday. As an InterContinental Ambassador and Diamond Elite IHG One Member, I have had the honor of visiting many great InterContinental Hotel properties globally. Coincidentally, 21 is also the number of InterContinental Hotels I have been to after my Chicago Birthday trip. This is the 21st InterContinental Property I have ever stayed at, and the Chicago Magnificent Mile Hotel has the honor of being named that for me. On top of celebrating my birthday, I wanted to try out the ongoing development of IHG One. My membership was changed from Spire Elite to Diamond Elite days before this trip, so I wanted to celebrate my loyalty brand’s new membership development by visiting an InterContinental Hotel. Also, it was my first time visiting Chicago since I was an infant, so I wanted to bring home full-minded recollections of the city as I travel the globe.

Much of my visit however felt underwhelming on a hard product and service level, which led me to raise my concerns in this review, but also talk about the great things about the Hotel. I came to stay at the Chicago Magnificent Mile InterContinental Hotel from April 15th to the 16th of 2022. It should be noted that I rely on using IHG as a form of booking the reservations, but also primarily rely on the hotel’s separate official website to help me set a guideline on my expectations.

I booked the Executive Tower King Classic Room, which is $16 more than th

One star for the great location and the bellhops.

In short, I don't recommend this hotel. (Not sure if it makes a difference, but I booked the room through Virgin America's site as part of a package rather than directly with the hotel.)

I checked in for a 3-night, 4-day stay at the Intercontinental. At first glance, this hotel seemed to be a great choice due to its location and the brand.

I was so wrong.

The front-desk staff was consistently unfriendly to the point of being rude whenever I talked to them. Two women helped me during the check-in process--one of them appeared to be in training and stared at me the entire time, unblinkingly, and without saying a word. I glanced her way a few times and made eye contact, thinking that would discourage the staring. Nope.

I was not given any information at all other than where my room was located.

In the elevator, a bellhop was in the middle of delivering luggage, and he very cheerfully gave me all the information the front-desk people should have: where the fitness center and pool are located and the hours for each, how to get there, etc. Very nice guy.

I got to my room and realized I got put in what is possibly the worst room on the entire 6th floor, if not the hotel. (Note: If you get room 667, ask for another one.) It felt TINY.

It's now 4 a.m. I am tired as I haven't slept in 48 hours. The problem? I couldn't fall asleep because the air conditioning was loud and apparently not working because the room was a stuffy 75 degrees despite my setting the thermostat to 65 degrees. I decided to turn off the AC and open the window (mind you, it's Chicago in late October--freezing), thinking I'd rather deal with cold and sleep with extra blankets then suffocate to death. Sweet, fresh air rolls in through the window, and temp in the room probably dropped to 55 degrees.

Should be able to sleep, right? I mean, a blanket cave would keep me nice and warm. NOPE. With the AC off, I could now hear a clunking noise. *CLUNK* ....

We checked into the Swissotel on June 30, 2008 without any issues. We were upgraded (with our Swiss Club Gold, get online for free use of gym/ pool otherwise $50 charge) to a corner room. We had a great view of Navy Pier and the Taste of Chicago.

The front desk staff was never very helpful when we had any requests for simple things. When does the shuttle leave for O'hare? How much is the shuttle? Why is the cleaning staff telling us they are out of coffee for the rooms? Can we borrow a screwdriver to fix our daughter's Gameboy? All of these were answered by blank stares and major confusion.

On July 4th, we were checking out. I needed to print out our boarding passes. The business center was closed due to the holiday. I went to the front desk to ask to have 5 minutes of access to the "Executive Level" business center to print our passes. At this point the only place open with internet access. I was told "no, it is an access only room". Huh? I explained what was going on and they could charge us whatever, we needed to print these passes. So the hunt for the key to the business office began. Now most normal people would have saved the hassle and sent us up to the Executive Floor. After 20 mins of not finding anyone with a key, guess where I ended up...the Executive Floor to print our boarding passes, totally silly. Just not a great overall feel from the front desk or the concierge.

The rooms were nice, clean and updated. The beds super comfortable.

BE ADVISED!!! There is major construction going on right behind the hotel at the new development AQUA. Non-stop from sun up to sun down. Drilling, pounding, ect. Did not bother us as we were pretty far away. We could really only hear in our bathroom and in lobby of hotel. However, we were never advised of this. Several other hotels also have major construction going on around them, so not a huge deal, no dust, which would have been a bummer.

Overall, our room was super cheap on Expedia. Would we stay again, if we could

I've been staying here for years, maybe twenty+, and don't always stay over on a Sunday, but I remember brunch being delicious, copious, with many choices and very tasty. Brunch this past weekend was dismal: runny bland eggs, limp bacon, soupy oatmeal, and the coffee was really off. I didn't really like anything I had, unfortunately, and have always looked forward to a brunch there before getting on the road if I was staying on a weekend. I also don't like the redecorated dining area--it's dark and lugubrious, makes me feel like I'm at somebody's funeral. My room on the business floor was terrific, though, and that would have gotten five stars.

I have spent 3 nights here on a biz trip along with a few colleagues. I am a Gold HHonors member. Check in was easy but my room on the executive level is tiny and has no view. The AC does not work, I can't get it below 70 degrees. When I asked, thre guy at the executive desk said to try to turn it from celcius to farenheit to get it to work. It worked for a few minutes. Can't sleep. There were one set of towels in the room. I couldn't find the hair dryer so I asked for another. Had to ask twice. there is a giant computer screen that comes on at all hours filled with banner ads and takes up most of the desk. housekeeping is very poor, and did not refill my coffee or cups on any of the days there are four small overused pillows for the king size bed and no extras. message light on phone is flashing to tell me a quick way to check out, dial a 4 digit number. I do, and the number does not work. It is closed. It's supposed to be automated system. Then good luck figuring out how to get the message deleted so the light doesn't flash all night. No prompts. I finally just pushed all the buttons and one of them worked. This is just one of those big historical hotels that doesn't pay attention to details. But at $300 a night we should expect a little more. I have to come back to chicago in a few weeks but I won't be staying here.

Stayed here recently with my husband when we were here for a concert – location was key as it was closet to marina & Northerly Island concert. Booked the same day, for the price & rating of the hotel I expected more. The hotel décor is beautiful & overall the room we had was lovely. It was one of the first hotels I’ve been to in the states that had a wood floor & not carpet. So smart & it had a much cleaner feeling to a room. Bathroom had a lovely large walk in shower & separate soaking tub. We had a balcony room & a “park – lake view” but the windows were absolutely filthy. We had one of the first floors of rooms, so the “view” was a lil bit of a stretch. Not a deal breaker as we are from Chicagoland.

I hate to be that reviewer but we became very disappointed, in the service. As we first arrived in our room, chilled there for an hour & then someone tried to enter our room and was able to get in but our inside door lock stopped them from getting in and they left. My husband went out to see who it was, it was housekeeping, she tried to come in our room – not sure if she thought we left already, forgot something or what happened – nothing else was needed in our room as we just checked in nor did we call for anything. We ordered room service, after we got back from the concert – which was later in the night. We realized that it was a late night menu, so options were slim and the person who took or order said it would take around 45 minutes. Almost 2 hours later, we still had not received our food. We literally fell asleep, woke up & called again about it. It was finally on its way. At that point, we were not even hungry anymore but for the time it took to get here I was expecting something amazing. The food came and it was disappointing. Bland tomato soup (Panera is better!), chicken wrap (with a semi stale wrap that clearly had been sitting out for too long), a mediocre salad & a lukewarm Pellegrino – well over $60 in room service food that was not even good. At that

I stayed at this location for the Marriott brand and location while working at a trade show for a week. Last year, it was a great experience overall. Last week was a DISASTER. The bellman, concierge and front desk staff are all very polite. That has not changed. A few of the waitstaff in the restaurant are exceptional as well. BUT, that is where it all ends. The bed is like sleeping on a brick. The pillows are uncomfortable. I had requested a mini refrigerator and foam pillows when making the reservation (verbal confirmation to my written request), but had to call down to the desk, as they had not been provided. TWO days, the room was NEVER touched. No cleaning, no replenishment of towels, toiletries, coffee, etc. I was out of the room from 7am until 10pm! NO excuses. I complained and the first night I was given 3,000 points in my account, an apology and offer to come clean the room (at that point 10:30pm) and a free cocktail in the bar if I waited downstairs for my room to be cleaned. I just asked for some towels and toiletries and went to bed. The second night they gave me a certificate for a free night, as it was already 11pm! I ate breakfast in the hotel restaurant one morning....what a mistake. My omlette and toast were burned. They took a while to be served as well. When I complained, they offered to make it again, but I had to leave for the trade show (I worked a booth). They still charged me! They took off 20%. I complained later to the front desk. They did not remove the charge. They gave me a coupon for a free breakfast that I never got to use :( Not enough? The elevators were quirky for 3 days and actually got stuck a few times for 5 minutes. next year, I think I am going to try a new location. There are plenty in the area in the same price range, some are better hotels. I was considering a weekend get-a-way here but not now.

The hotel is excellently situated for visiting The Art Institute or Millennium Park. It is an "old classic" of a hotel, and the main lobby is a must see, but I would not stay there again. The problem with any hotel dating from long ago (the current one was built in 1923-25) is that, even with extensive remodeling (which was done in 2007-2009) it is hard to create spaces that are comparable to what is expected today (in many cases the walls are load bearing so cannot be changed too much). We had a reasonably sized room (king bed and space for a desk and lounge chair) but the bathroom was very small with little space to spread out toiletries. Also, there was no coffee machine in the room - something I have come to expect in virtually all hotels these days. My major complaint about this stay was the Executive Lounge. It is usually worth while to pay the extra for access - in most hotels it is not much more than the cost of two breakfasts and there are usually some hors d'oeuvres in the evening along with a modest wine at a modest price. (Since we are currently at the diamond level in the Hilton Honors program, we get access for free at the Hiltons but I usually upgrade at other chains such as the Hyatt.) At the Palmer Executive Lounge I ordered a couple of glasses of wine (there was a bartender on duty) and was slightly surprised by the cost - $9/glass which came to $21.44 for two with tax and tip - and was even more surprised by the wine. It was about the worst wine I had tasted in many years. I didn't think it was even as good as Trader Joe's cheapest $2.99 wine that I tried a couple of weeks ago. Unfortunately I didn't get a look at the bottle to see what it was (although I did notice that she had to fish it out of the back of a cupboard). I noticed that a couple of groups in the executive lounge had brought their own wine in - and I can understand iot at the prices they were charging. It then occurred to me that the wine had been sitting around open for some time.
Just can't sleep at this hotel. This was our last stay. We've stayed four times in the past two years and the outcome is always the same. Too much darn noise on the streets, can't sleep.

Its very expensive for not much going on in the rooms. They charged for parking 25 dollars a day. That 25 plus the 200 dollars a night was ridiculous. All this hotel offers is location. On average this hotel is maybe worth 250 for the weekend. The wifi was the worst wifi I have ever used. We might as well have had dial up.

I was really shocked by this hotel.

I live in Chicago and was familiar with the property, having passed it many times and heard about its restaurant and rooftop bar. It seemed like a pretty cool boutique hotel, and its location, right on Lincoln Park, is a nice departure from the typical Loop/Gold Coast/Magnificent Mile hotel.

So, excited for a little staycation during the pandemic, I made a reservation, genuinely excited to test-out the place, especially since Joie de Vivre hotels had joined Hyatt, a brand I really trust.

The hotel was disgusting. I don’t just mean not to my personal taste, or a little worn around the edges, etc. It was shocking.

My room (a suite!) was in horrible condition; the actual condition of the room compared to the photographs of the room online was laughable. The photos online show a fluffy, comfortable couch with multiple pillows; I had a dumpy, faded couch with no pillows that looked like it belonged in a college dorm. The photos online show a comfortable looking bed with a chic headboard and cute artwork; I had a rock hard mattress, an unevenly hung headboard, and no art. The tiny desk in my room had tons of nicks and peeling stain. The paint on the walls and floorboards was chipped and peeling. The heater was an ancient box beneath the window that burst out intense bouts of heat, with great noise, right next to the desk—followed by bouts of no heat. The window shades were grimy. The bathroom was tiny; the paint on the ceiling had bubbles all over it from the humidity and lack of a vent. And the carpet...yuck...my socks got dusty from walking on it, and it was covered with stains.

Common areas were in no better shape: the elevator had broken tiles and Formica, the soda machine areas on the floor had broken tiles, the hallways had dirty carpet (and low, drop ceilings from yesteryear). The only thing that looked halfway decent about the place was the lobby.

Service was underwhelming. The employee at check-in might best be described

I had heard great things about this hotel and although I have never stayed there, I am sure the rooms are great, but the rooftop bar was a major disappointment. We then retreated down to the Common Club which was a nice bar but the service was terrible. Needless to say I won’t be returning.

I've stayed at this hotel several times. I checked out this morning for what is likely the last time. The layout of the hotel is ridiculous. You have to take an elevator up several stories to the lobby and then walk nearly a full city block to a second set of elevators to get to your room. The layout would be tolerable if it weren't for the never ending crowds and long lines to get on and off the elevator. The breakfast and reception were so over crowded that I was never able to to take advantage of either. The lines were crazy long. The manager and front desk worker were also very rude upon check-in. I was with a colleague and it was shortly after noon. My room was ready but his was not. This wouldn't have been an issue if they had handled it well. Instead, they both rudely kept saying that check-in time was 3:00pm. A simple "we'll work as quickly as possible to get a room for you" would have been just fine. Lastly, the room itself was older with holes in the wall from where a picture was apparently forcibly removed, and the toilet would not flush. I notified maintenance and they did fix it, sort of, although you had to really work at juggling the handle to get the water to stop running. The location is fine but unfortunately I'll take my business elsewhere from now on.

Long time Trip Advisor reader, first time contributer. My wife and I decided to spend our wedding anniversary in the city where we got engaged. We go to Chicago every summer and have stayed at many different hotels in Chicago. We arrived late on a Friday night. Traffic was hectic and my wife and I were having a hard time finding which parking garage the Embassy Suites uses. After circling the block many times we called the front desk and and explained our dilemma. The lady we spoke to was very rude and seemed bothered by our phone call. I could only hope this call was not setting the tone for our weekend at this property. Thanks to the very polite valet guy we were able to find the proper entrance to the parking garage.
Checking in was quick and easy. We grabbed our keys and headed to our room. At this point we were exhausted from the drive and the stress of finding the parking garage! We unpacked, watched a little tv and went to sleep.
When we woke up the next morning we noticed the vent in the bathroom had a thick layer of dust on it. I am sensitive to dust so my headaches and congestion now made sense. There were hairs in the shower and a stain near the shower drain. We requested a new room but were considering checking out and finding somewhere else to go!
The manager I spoke to was very kind and offered to switch our room. As we were packing, another very kind lady came to our door to see the issues we discussed. She was understanding and apologized. We received our new room keys and moved our stuff to the new room. The smell of the room was much better..I don't know if they freshened it up before we got there but it was nice. As we planned out our day someone knocked on the door and delivered us sparkling wine, strawberries, assorted candy and an anniversary card. What a nice gesture and it was greatly appreciated.
We spent the rest of the day out and then went to see a movie at the movie theater that is in the same building as the hotel! We fell asleep as soo

I have stayed here a half dozen times and I think this is my last. The place really declined. First, my private room was filthy, looked like it hadn't been swept or vacuumed in months. The showers has mold all over the walls. No hot water in the sink that barely works. Filthy window sills. And someone's bra was under the bed along with fingernail clippings and garbage. Second, the shared bathrooms had dust Buffaloes throughout and stunk. Third the property is really let go. Front entrance the cement is crumbling and a mess. Windows are filthy. Really disappointed and to to top it off they upped there prices to unbelievable. Stay at the Inn at Lincoln Park for the same price.

Rooms are outdated and unclean. Rooms need to be completely renovated. Furniture is worn, wallpaper has been torn and repaired in a number of places. Corners were dirty in the bathrooms especially. Hair in the bathtub when we arrived and the bedroom was clearly not vaccumed. Our original room was on the 12th floor that looked at an empty building. There was no view whatsoever. When we addressed this with the front desk, our only option was to move to a King bed as opposed to a double bed suite. My daughter and I had to sleep together in order to get the view we were expecting. Overall staff was very friendly and accomodating but will stay somewhere else on my return to the city.

We stayed at the Waldorf Astoria for one evening to celebrate our 25th wedding anniversary in Chicago. The Waldorf certainly made this an anniversary we'll remember.

We booked through AMEX Fine Resorts and Hotels. We chose the Waldorf over the Peninsula (where we've stayed before) since AMEX informed us that the Peninsula was under renovation. Since we were only staying one evening, we wanted to be assured of a pleasant stay.

Upon check-in, there is a sign that alerts you to the fact they have construction as well. The person at check in informed us that we were high enough up to avoid most of the noise. Not close. Hammer drilling that was either on our floor or the floor above made it difficult to make a phone call. The fire detector went off numerous times in our room. We did not have TV most of our stay. When we arrived back to our room after dinner, the smoke detector went off again and we had to change rooms.

Some of the staff did the best they could under the circumstance they were dealt. Dennis, the Guest Services Manager, should be immediately promoted for being on the front lines of this mess of a hotel. Some of the staff seemed to simply ignore the problems. If you're going to be under renovation, inform future guests like the Peninsula did and let us make a decision if we want to stay there or choose a different hotel. Whoever is responsible for the decision not to communicate this to us or AMEX should have to deal with the aftermath of furious guests. We were on a first name basis with the building engineer he visited our room so often.

We ordered breakfast from room service. If you want water, be sure to specify that. Our meal came and water or cream for Oatmeal were not included.

The experience could not have been any worse. There was absolutely nothing good about what happened here. You only get one opportunity to celebrate a special occasion like ours and they simply blew it terribly. I asked to speak with the GM upon our departure and could not

This is a crappy excuse for a waldorf…more on the lines of an upscale garden inn or holiday inn. We arrived early but wanted to shop small business Saturday so we were good with waiting for our room - after being told we could upgrade for an extra 300 a night. That’s an upsell not an upgrade. One is for a price and one is complementary (usually based on brand loyalty (I have over a million Hilton points) and I’ve been a diamond member for 7 years)). They were kind enough to upgrade us to a room with a fireplace but…..We were told it should be an hour. 2.5 hours later, we were summoned back to the hotel and given a room that had one lamp on one side of the bed, a mini fridge sitting in the middle of the room (I was afraid to open it) and no desk lamp. Pics attached. The second room also had no desk lamp and severely scuffed furniture, holes in the wall and stained carpet and…the fireplace which is the reason for the increased cost/upgrade - doesn’t work. Pics attached. Highlights are the amenities (the ones accessible not the ones promised but never allowed to use) - the gym and spa facilities, the concierge who was wonderful, the bar. The bellman and valet were unavailable for the first 24 hours of our stay - no where to be found to help us with luggage or direct traffic in the very small drop off area. No hailing of taxis or advice where to pick up an Uber (the answer is on the street and not at the lobby entrance).

And this is just day one of two nights.

I find it difficult to judge the value of the room we had in this hotel because I usually stay with friends when I am in the Bay Area and I therefore have little recent experience of SF hotels. However, I was disappointed since most of your reviewers like this hotel a lot. I have to say in fairness that the main problem we experienced may have been peculiar to the room we had. I was very tired when we arrived and the first night I slept until 5 am when I was awakened by an irritating hummng noise but I didn't worry too much since it went away at about 6 am. However, the next night I was awakened in the early hours by the same noise and we got little sleep for the rest of the night. I tried unplugging various things but to no effect and I eventually decided that the noise was coming from an electrical device outside the room and located behind the bed. Again it went away at 6 am. I would have asked for a room change but we were leaving that morning. At departure I mentioned our bad experience to the staff and they said they would certainly have given us a room change, but I am sure they were aware of the problem with that room. As for the rest of the hotel I can say that it was satisfactory: our room wasn't particularly small, and the decor was OK except for the carpet which had seen better days and which had some strange white marks on it as though someone had tried to clean it with bleach. There were no coffee making facilities in the room but coffee was freely available in the lobby. The location of the hotel was wonderful. My only other gripe is that about a month before our stay I tried to book a room online using the hotel website but I was told that there were none available but because of the reviews the hotel receives on Tripadvisor I persisted and I obtained a room through an agent but this may have increased the cost - the Chancellor was the most expensive hotel on my recent visit to California. I would only say to anyone thinking of staying at the Chancel

We booked this hotel based on what Priceline showed us and all the pictures are fake. Beware of this hotel if you can. The older female manager with the men's haircut is totally rude and treats people horrible. 20 rooms have to share one bathroom and the toilets are disgusting. Additionally there are roaches crawling on the floors. We checked in and checked out in one hour with no refund. Book at your own risk!

The hotel lobby was under renovation during our stay making for first impression less than favorable. The room itself needed updating. The windows in the bathroom opened to other facing rooms. The 'English' breakfast was not worth the effort to go. Would not recommend or stay at this hotel again.

For me, hotels are fairly simple, provide a safe, friendly, and clean place to sleep. The staff at Parc 55 were professional, friendly, and welcoming. Room cleanliness was just OK. There were two long hairs stuck to the wall of the bathroom along with what looked like someone had picked their nose and wiped it on the wall. The rest of the room seemed very clean, but it made me wonder about the attention to detail. The reason for the poor review is that we were evacuated from the hotel in the middle of the night on two of our three nights. I know that alarms are inconvenient and for the safety of the guests. I understand that the hotel cannot control guests doing stupid things and accidentally or maliciously activating an alarm, but two nights in a row without an explanation other than "false alarm?" Without any further explanation I have to believe that something is screwed up with the hotel's fire system. I am disappointed that our sleep was severely disrupted on two of three nights with no apology or even a decent explanation.

We decided to post a review mid-stay. We have not completed our stay here yet, however, I must say that this hotel does not come close to meeting our expectations. We even feel a bit uneasy giving a bad review because we are not picky travellers and have never had an experience like this. Throughout this California trip alone, we have stayed in 1-star motor inns, 4 star upscale hotels, and others in between. We have not had anything bad to say about any of our previous acconomodations, let alone what we have already experienced here.

Our first encounter was with the valet parking service gentleman. I asked him if I could park my own car in the space as we had a lot of luggage that would require multiple trips. We also had some loose items that we wanted to pack away before unloading. He could barely speak English and just kept saying "NO, I PARK, NO I PARK, YOU NO PARK". We come from Toronto and are very used to multi-culturalism, however, we could not understand a word he was saying. I again asked him if I can park myself, and he said "NO, YOU PAY $38 PER NIGHT AND I PARK". We unloaded all of our luggage onto the driveway and gave him the keys. If he would have explained it's valet-only, it would have made more sense to me. He could also have offered to get someone to assist us with our bags. From there, we were standing in the parking garage without any idea of how to enter the hotel or reach the elevator. Very poor service and a frustrating/awkward conversation. Especially when it's your first impression.

The front-desk lady was very pleasant and professional. She promptly gave us a run down on the hotel as she quickly checked us in. However, when we got to our room and turned on the A/C, it started dripping down water. Unfortunately, the vent is on the ceiling right by the door so we got the moldy water on us. Yes, moldy water. Looking up into the vent, there were green and brown spots of mold from where it was consistently dripping down. We called the front d

While the location, room and amenities were all good to great at the Handlery, my check in experience left a dark cloud over the rest of my stay that never dissipated. I booked the Rooftop Garden Suite at the Handlery San Francisco for 2012-03-03. I had considered booking the previous day (Friday) but sadly it was more expensive and already booked, besides which I had a previous engagement. When I arrived at the hotel to check in, the staff were unable to locate my reservation. After some back room consultations, the front desk rep produced records showing that I hadn't checked in the previous night, and so my reservation had been canceled and I'd been charged for a "no show". Of course, I hadn't checked in the previous night because I'd been several hundred miles away in my own bed, exactly as planned. After another fifteen minute wait, the rep informed me that the suite was free tonight (obviously a surprise, given I'd booked it) and he'd be happy to rent it to me for an additional two hundred dollars. (This actually ended up coming out to about $260 because of something about pre-discount taxes and surcharges), so I ended up out of pocket about $625 for one night in this admittedly rather nice room. But not $625 nice. Not even close. I was very very tempted to cancel my week long stay with Handlery after this experience, but the difficulty of finding a better place to stay during the Game Developers Conference was daunting. I know for certain though that unless things have changed significantly I will not be returning to Handlery San Francisco or visiting Handlery San Diego any time soon, including for next year's GDC.

Of course, I hadn't checked in the previous night because I'd been several hundred miles away in my own bed, exactly as planned.
