(This actually ended up coming out to about $260 because of something about pre-discount taxes and surcharges), so I ended up out of pocket about $625 for one night in this admittedly rather nice room. But not $625 nice. Not even close. I was very very tempted to cancel my week long stay with Handlery after this experience, but the difficulty of finding a better place to stay during the Game Developers Conference was daunting. I know for certain though that unless things have changed significantly I will not be returning to Handlery San Francisco or visiting Handlery San Diego any time soon, including for next year's GDC.

i really wanted to love this place but the little things kept me from giving a higher score. the rooms are nice and clean but weird out-croppings (support beams?) from the wall which tells you that the refurb was limited to space. do not leave the key in fridge door, you will hit it when you awake...4 bandages on my knee can attest. our room (404) was directly next to the ymca and you literally hear them play ball because by the squeaks from the hard wood floors. scared to nap for 2x staff would open door after the 2nd knock to drop off suit and check if room has been cleaned. centrally located if you want to gnosh at boulevard or see a film at the embarq. but I advise that if you really want to stay at a Kimpton...stay at the palomar.

Prior to my stay I attempted to get in touch and called to no avail. Probably made 10 calls, a few were answered and immediately hung up without even a hello. A few were answered and after a sentence or two of conversation I was hung up on. A few times a never ending machine and hold, no one ever answered. Finally got fed up and the next person who answered I asked to speak with a manager. They transferred me to what became an asserting machine that had no greeting so I didn't know who I would have left a message for. Absolutely ridiculous how impossible it is to get a hold of someone. Location is decent, but should definitely be called the Hilton Chinatown,

We upgraded to get a view of the Bay Bridge. This meant we had a corner room. The room was nice. The walk to get there was difficult with numerous blind corners on a narrow pathway. The room itself had a lot of unused space. Grand entrance but tiny bathroom. There was a door opening into the room and the shower door opening into the room. One door had to be shut in order to open the other and you had to maneuver around the toilet. Not well planned at all. It does feel secure as the room key operates the elevator. There was construction going on. One of the mornings we had coffee served on the second floor. You had to take the elevator down and then go up two escalators. The next day I went to the same place and the coffee was served in the lobby. Back down the two escalators... only to find the coffee gone. Waited fifteen minutes and gave up. We did like the location and walked to the Ferry building for Farmers Market on Saturday and to and from the Giants games for both day games and night games. Felt safe.

The check in was great the hotel staff was amazing and super friendly and helpful. The rooms are outdated not what i would expect from a marriot more like a super 8. I kept encountering large wet spots in the halls on floor 6. The lobby looks great the food is okay but with the most ridiculous prices imaginable. Many hotels give a complimentary breakfast while this one charges you $32 for it. Run away from
this breakfast as far as you can a large spread but nothing to brag about at all. I have paid less per person for nicer hotels to deliver my breakfast to my room. There are so many great places within walking distance to get better food for less than half the price. I also felt sick after eating the breakfast, not sure if that was the food or the price. I will do more research next time i come to san francisco and probably wont stay here. When i go to a hotel i like it to be nicer than my home so i feel like im on vacation, this hotel did not fit that bill.

The hotel itself is ok...rooms are nice and have been renovated...parking is ridiculously expensive, front desk service is terrible..location is good if you like to be around the piers and wharfs..I would not stay here again.

Unfortunately, Fairmont San Francisco leaves much to be desired. For a world-class luxury hotel, world-class service was seriously lacking at the Fairmont San Francisco.

First, within 20 minutes of entering the room, there were two separate visits by hotel staff. First was a technician coming to check on the drapes - apparently he had the wrong room number. The second visit was by another staff who wanted to take inventory of the snack and drinks bar. I have never ever experienced something like that, let alone a high-end property like the Fairmont San Francisco. I went to the front desk and confirmed that the room was in fact "ready." It appears that communications were seriously lacking at the Fairmont San Francisco.

The communications problem was further highlighted when, on the day of my departure, housekeeping staff came to my door and wanted to enter. I told them that I had been granted late checkout at 3pm but she said the front desk told her that I was checking out at 1:30pm. I also noticed that a porter was sent to a wrong room on my floor to retrieve luggage of a departing guest. Four miscommunications over the course of one day are inexcusable, especially for a Fairmont.

We also dined at the Laurel Court restaurant twice, once for dinner and once for the Sunday brunch. While the dinner service was good, the Sunday brunch service also left much, much to be desired. We were told by the hostess that we could not have coffee while we waited because the only server for that side of the restaurant was too busy. I could not believe what I was hearing. I have never, ever been given that as an excuse. We were told to go to Cafe Centro to get our own coffee. Mind you that the Laurel Court was half empty when we were "waiting" for a table to open up. Apparently the wait was just due to the lack of staff. We ended up sitting in the empty half of the restaurant.

I’m staying at the hotel right now, being charged 330 per night for the room having decided to treat myself to a nice room in the city as a way to relax after a medical procedure today.

Check in took approx 25 minutes as there were only 2 staff checking a line of guests in. That was after using the online checkin that was pointless as, upon completing that, i got an email telling me to go to the front desk upon arrival.

I called at approx 9pm tonight asking for an extra blanket. It eventually arrived at almost 1130, after multiple follow up calls. I don’t even know why the rooms don’t have proper blankets now the first place given the hotel is very cold.

70 dollars per night for parking is beyond absurd.

No bars or restaurants are open nor is there any water in the rooms - in order to get water, one has to rejoin the checkin line. There is a cafe that is open until 10 selling little else but pasta and a pathetic selection of snacks at absurd prices.

To charge 4 and 5 star prices while providing zero service is nothing short of a rip off.

The location is great! Used the digital key for only the second time. The notes received with the digital check in where well done, only thing would be to explain the tower numbers and the room locations. I did get keys half way through the trip. I had issues with coming in late and a security guard not wanting to let me in because I didn't have a plastic key. Still had issues after showing him the digital key. The digital key did stop working part way through the trip but I did have the plastic keys. Room was older but the bed was comfortable and the room was clean. Also had issues with getting the correct member level benefits, received vouchers for the store instead of the restaurant. The front desk did resolve the issue. Not sure if I would stay here again maybe check out the new Hampton or the Pac 55 if you want the union area if not Hilton San Francisco is a better hotel but not as good as a location but still close. I think the hotel might be too big to really get any good service or benefits.

I stay at this hotel regularly, although I am not an IHG loyalist. I specifically liked this property. However, on a recent visit I requested assistance with an iPhone charger and was met with bewilderment. This is a basic request at any hotel, and I was willing to purchase but was told they could not provide me with one, and could not recommend a solution until the next morning despite this being San Francisco and presumably there are many 24 hour stores here. Very disappointed in the level of service as most hotels have standing stocks of chargers available for free, and others in this category make them available for purchase. In this instance I had brought 5 bookings in addition to my own and was very disappointed that they could not provide a $15 charger as a courtesy. Poor service overall.

Overall a D+ Grade! Although this is a new hotel and sister to the Intercontinental Mark Hopkins where we've stayed previously, this property has far too many downsides, especially for the prices they charge! First, upon checking in they didn't have our 1-King suite so we had to pay the same $ for get 2 adjoining regulars premier bedrooms! On a different yet related, in addition to the above hotel, we've also a stayed at the Fairmont, Westin Market, St Regis, and a number of other S.F. hotels of similar cost and caliber in the past 2-3 years and in comparison, for this Intercontinental property we would give: D+ for service (3 instances of in-room dinning and each time a major problem: 1st time, completely lost our order only try to reproduce after 1:10 later when complained for not having received; no silverware the 2nd time; double charged the 3rd time; housekeeping didn't change the obviously stained top flat sheet over the bed that accidentally got coffee on; the coffee, sugar, and creams were never restocked after the 1st day even though we were there for 3 night). Bathroom/shower: D+ grade: 27th floor room we were in had the weakest shower pressure we've experienced in 20+ of vacationing which we do about 5-6/year; the sliding pocket door handle was so sharp that simply grabbing and normal pulling on it to open the door caused a 1/2" cut and notable bleeding of my index finger. Noise-vise: although opposite to and 2 rooms over from the elevators, we could hear the whining of the elevator cable system all night which is annoying and disruptive enough to keep us up... Pool: cloudy waters and being used for a class for kids! Paying $400+ a night on Labor Day weekend we simply expect more from accommodations to be this poor! Spa: smaller but nice accommodation and service there but unless booked for massage a week in advance, they only had one (1) 50 minute opening available for the entire Friday-Monday Labor Day weekend! So if like us, at least one of your reason

My wife has been staying regularly at the Mark Hotel for several years and I occasionally stay there with her as well. We booked last minute to see a show and were graciously put into a large 2 bedroom suite as the UN meeting was ready to take place. The room was really a nice apartment, kitchen and laundry facility included.

Unfortunately, a certain delegation including the Emir of XXX (I use xxx to protect the privacy of the Emir) had over 40 rooms of the hotel, and while the hotel is a Non Smoking property, they chose to smoke. We even had a lady ( not sure if she was with The Mark or the delegation) tell us it was the sweet smell of hookahs. Security was stationed by the elevator of the floor, and when my wife mistakenly thought he was a guest and stated that the smell of the smoke was terrible, the agent replied " are you telling me." And the next day we encountered the same agent in the elevator on a different floor and he asked if it had gotten any better?

It is obvious that this is standard behavior which we have previously encountered at other hotels ( see my Leela Palace, New Delhi Review) and hotels are willing to break the law, turn their head, and inconvenience guests for the large sums that these people spend. This went on day and night and sleeping was difficult since the AC did not run as much due to the cooler night time weather.

Had this situation not occurred, I would give this property a 5 star review because that is what it truly deserves. But when management accepts this type of behavior in return for the large sums of money and states that they try to stop the smoking but they are not successful, I cannot accept this.

We did our annual holiday trip to NYC and decided to try a different hotel from our traditional plaza. I would have to say it was very disappointing. First we had my sons iPad taken “mistakenly “ from the room and had to escalate to security. It was found after several hours but no apology at all when it was found that the employee put it on their cart I felt as if they didn’t believe me that it was gone

Service was mediocre at best and nothing like what we are used to at same priced hotels 1300 us per night.

Family was disappointed in the stay, it’s too bad as it’s a beautiful property very well located

we stayed in the new Yorker again the last 3 times were good but this time very poor ,on checking in I asked for a room with a view this not very helpful woman checked us in the room was awful no view at all so back down I went and asked for a different room , which I got it was a little better but not much the bathroom was so tiny it was terrible the sink was all modern with a tap stuck in the middle you could just about get your hands in it .Since the wyndham had got involved it seems to have gone backwards and they called it re modernised its worse and we had an early check out and got a really unhelpful woman with a sour face like she had been sucking lemons , we will no be staying there again most unhappy

Our family stayed at the New Yorker this year; we are volunteers in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade, and the New Yorker was the headquarters for parade morning. Very handy to be right there.

The hotel has been steadily improving in the last couple years, and we had a New Yorker Suite with two double beds and a separate living room with a pull-out couch. Rather small room, but certainly serviceable (and affordable). Very new, very modern and comfortable.

My only complaint is with the shower - it is badly designed (and I fear they've now used the same design in all their remodeled rooms). The shower floor slopes AWAY from the floor drain, meaning that it always stays wet, with about 1/2" of water. But the worst part is the shower door: it is mounted above a 4" ledge that is supposed to contain the water. But the ledge slopes AWAY from the shower and towards the floor. Which means any water that accumulates on the ledge flows directly onto the floor as soon as you open the door. IDIOTIC. It's like the guys who built the bathroom got the instructions reversed. The shower floor should slope TOWARDS the drain, and the ledge beneath the shower door should slope TOWARDS the inside of the shower.

I fear that they are going to end up with a lot of water damage to the room, from the leaking showers right above. By the time four of us were done taking showers, there was nearly 1" of water on the bathroom floor: we ended up using virtually all our bath towels just to mop up the flow.

Otherwise a great hotel: location, price and comfort. But their idiotic shower designs will end up ruining the place. I've given the hotel a terrible rating, in the hope that management will read this review. There was no way to reach management when we stayed at the hotel, and no way to reach customer service from the website. And forget trying to get somebody in management when you call on the phone; this is New York, after all, and they don't care.

I totally did not enjoy my experience with this hotel. About the only true positive I can say about this hotel is the location, which is really good if you want to stay close to NYC’s sights like The Empire State Building, Chrysler Building, Grand Central Terminal or the Flatiron Building. There are a lot of restaurants, bars and shopping options around. I would recommend visiting all the Korean restaurants around the hotel. Food there is super delicious! The hotel is located inside of a cute looking historic building. The hotel’s lobby is pretty. I did not like the elevator. It is tiny and slow. Stairs are super uncomfortable the way they were built. In general, it feels tight and uncomfortable inside of the hotel. I also noticed dirty dusty carpets on my floor. My room (#307) was super small! I booked a Deluxe King room which was supposed to have extra-space, but I did not get this extra spacing at all. God only knows how catastrophically small the standard room is! One other plus I can mention is the room was perfectly clean, also everything looked pretty updated. The room faced inside of a hotel’s square so it was super dark inside my room all the time! Plus, I could clearly see what was going on in the windows opposite of mine, I bet these people could clearly see me in my room too. So you better keep the curtains closed all the time. My King Size bed was not really comfortable. There were not many plugs in the room to charge gadgets. No plugs next to the bed, a couple plugs were hidden behind a bed from one side. I did not like the little closet, as there was not even enough space there to put one big piece of luggage. So finally my luggage had to be placed next to the bed on the floor. The bathroom was also super small. I liked having a bathtub, but it could not fit me (I am 6 feet tall). Also the toilet was right next to the bathtub, and I found this design super weird. I had a cat with me, and I barely could find some space in the bathroom to fit a cat’s li

Just tried to get into this place again. I have tried numerous times this year, but it is always full.

Want to stay just one night??? Pay the $85.00 fee to 'clean the room'. I paid all of this money for this? Really? Again, i will just stat at the Andaz....

The rooms are clean, and well designed. Great beds, nice linens...all that is fine.

Service? Sorry, but this staff does NOT compare to the staff at the old club in the Hilton Hotel in anyway. They don't have the sincerity, or the warm passion. I miss John and the old club staff. This place has a typical "NYC" attitude, and that is not a compliment!

If you want to BUY something here...they all jump! They are great at reminding you that you can buy more points. They are great at sending little cards, and setting up sales appointments. THey are great at bothering you with phone calls under the pretense of welcoming you to West 57th, and then trying to schedule an appt. to 'review' your membership (sell you more). Unfortunately, they are NOT great at being able to fulfill your reservation needs. OVERSOLD!!

I am very sorry i ever bought here. This is without a doubt, the WORST investment i have ever made. I would love to debate their sales staff on this now...after the fact. It all looks great on paper...during the sales process, but it is NOT!

I have tried repeatedly to use my ownership investment to stay here. I cannot get in. If you have 6 months to 12 months to plan your trip, you might be 'ok'. If you need to plan with less than 4 weeks notice, and stay on a weekend.... Forget it. Not going to happen.

They try to get you to use your points to stay at a hilton hotel....HORRIBLE value for your points.

The only good thing i have to say about this is that the breakfast and evening cocktail service in the owners lounge in lovely. The staff is great, the food is nice...great job. It also has nothing to do with Hilton...these folks are 'third party'....from a fabulous restaurant down the street. Great job, team.

I liked the location of the hotel, not too far from Times Square. I liked that they had a Keurig machine, but you have to request coffee daily. I found the following drawbacks, and therefore would not return to this hotel: no mirror except in bathroom
- bathroom sliding door doesn't close completely, so there's no privacy.
There's an opaque glass wall between bathroom and room. When someone uses the bathroom, the opaque glass wall transmits light into hotel room.
- walls are so thin that you can hear someone coughing in their room. The security knocked on our door at night to tell us to stop talking because there was a complaint. Neither TV was on, nor music, and nor laughing. Simple talking results in a visit from the security guard! Felt like I was in a library and had to whisper.

Booked this hotel through booking.com, it was meant to be a celebration of our 40th wedding anniversary. After a long day traveling from the southwest of Ireland we arrived at hotel at 6 pm, we had estimated on our booking that we’d arrive between 6 and 7 pm. We were greeted with sorry we canceled your room, card declined used as the excuse.This was a lie, plus we were to pay on arrival as on our booking stated. Ended up stressed, disappointed, stayed in another hotel that was terrible, receptionist booked a private taxi for us to get there. which cost $65 , should have cost $20. We were vulnerable and they knew it. Hotel didn’t treat us well, we have emailed hotel and emailed manager privately but didn’t get a reply, very bad manners. Not even an acknowledgement. I work in the tourist industry in Ireland and we know how to treat our tourists after all its because of them I have a job.

I was very very disappointed, first time in my life to see a 4star hotel with only a shower without a bath tab. Rooms are very very very small for a couple. Their queen bed seemed like a single bed to me way too small. The service was poor from entering a hotel, no one to assist with luggage, the hotel stuff starred at us as if were distracting them. It was a pity. I wonder where they get all this good reviews?

Currently staying at hotel. Arrived early, so room was not available. Certainly understood. Was told they would call cell when room was available. No call. Had pre ordered wine and water for room. When I arrived in room - nothing was there. I called to complain - and never really got an explanation - only a mild apology. Arrived back to the room this evening - the room key did not work. After getting the third version of the room key - and kind of a condescending tutorial on how they work, the key did access the room. I am quite an experienced traveler - and found the comments insulting. I am only a Gold preferred member of Hilton Honors (previous Diamond). After deciding to leave my Platinum status as Starwood to re-achieve Diamond - am very much second guessing my decision (3 out of my last 4 stays at Hilton properties have been far below the standard).

The hotel itself is nice and well positioned. The service, however, is substandard. I'm a Platinum Ambassador and a frequent guest to ICs, so I have a good basis for the comparison.

An express FedEx delivery was sent to me and the hotel signed for it. When I called guest services, they did not have a record of my shipment, so I had to tell them who signed for it. In total, it took five phone calls and over an hour for the parcel to delivered to my room. I was late for an important meeting because of that. When the parcel was finally delivered, the guy said "good morning", handed me the package, turned around and left. Now, I'm not big on hello's and goodbye's and sorry's, but I somehow thought something was out of the ordinary for an IC charging $300-400 and above per night.

On another occasion, I tried to phone the concierge, but they were always busy, and I was redirected to guest services, who told me that this is a question for the concierge, so they transfered me there (without saying "I will transfer you"), and guess what -- busy again. Of course, the guest services had to know that the concierge is likely busy, meaning they did not care about solving my problem, they just cared about getting rid of me. The exact same thing happened twice, and on the second try I left a message for the concierge with my question. Do you want to guess what happened? No response, of course. Being understaffed is just not an excuse for a supposed IC property.

If you have a "do not disturb" sign on your door, don't count on it. The room service will still knock and ask you when the room can be serviced.

The bell staff can be very friendly and helpful, but can ignore you at times, too busy with conversations of their own.

The breakfast is also sub-standard with poor selection. You would do much better at one of the Hiltons.

At check-out, I complained and an Assistant Front Office Manager (M. C. S.) came, who assured me that she would "investigate" the matter. She did look gen

Hi
We recently stayed at the intercontinental Times Square in New York , what a disappointment. We booked this from a search looking for 5 star accomodation and it was not that.
From the time we arrived it was downhill from there we booked one of the king rooms on the 36th floor and it was so noisy all night traffic noise police cars etc. we asked to be moved the next morning to a quieter part of the hotel so they said we will put you on the other side which will be quieter, this was not the case.
Then there's the room , it was clean but definitely could do with maintenance the room was supposed to be a serviced room. When we stay in 5 Star in Hong Kong or Japan the whole room is cleaned from top to bottom at the intercontinental we were in they chose what they did on certain days, some days rubbish bins were not emptied or rubbish left on counter tops in bedroom on 3 occasions the bin in the bathroom was not emptied and the shower was not cleaned. Normally you get complimentary bottled water each day but no instead we were charged $10.50 per bottle which was around 350mls very small and a complete ripoff.
I found that some of the staff were very rude.
On checking out they tried to charge us for cashew nuts and extra bottles of water which we did not have and she said well you must of moved them as they are weighted so if you pick them up we charge your room. Again we explained we did not have them my partner she said I did pick up a couple of things on the tray to look at them but put them back again. The lady replied well from now on if you pick them up then we will presume you have taken them and you will be charged.
Very rude. We travel 4 times a year around the USA and Asia and I would never book at an in continental hotel again
I would rate this hotel in time square no better than a 3 star. I do not know how they get away with advertising as a 5 star thus is false advertising.
Unfortunately as we had prepaid we could not leave and go elsewhere
I would strongly

Recently stayed in the Intercontinental Times Square while travelling for business. The hotel was in a great spot, close to Times Square but far enough away so there wasn’t too much noise. However was let down completely by the service. Our booking was incorrect on arrival (only 2 rooms showing instead of the 3 we had booked) and the staff were not willing to help until we gave them a print out of the original booking. When we were finally given rooms, we were told to come down the following morning and check if there were any better (King) rooms available, and when I went down the following morning the staff were incredibly rude and no help at all. Then, to top it all off, I was out all of the following day and came home to a hotel room that was completely unserviced. Would not stay here again as there are plenty of other great hotels in the vicinity.

The worst thing about the hotel is the location. Times Square is probably the last neighborhood in Manhattan that should have a luxury hotel. Do a web search; most luxury hotels had the sense to avoid Times Square, and for good reason. Luxury hotels are supposed to be about good-taste, refinement, nuance and serenity. Times Square is the polar-opposite of all of that.

The ground floor tenant is a Shake Shack, which continuously has a line extending out the door and into the sidewalk. The queue is full of with rubes who are afraid to venture outside the tourist-zone for an overrated burger. Here's a note to any NYC first-time visitor: The burger isn't that good, but if you must try one, there are franchises in Brooklyn and the Upper East Side, or Philadelphia for that matter, that have no line at all (venture off the beaten path, you might learn something).

The base of the building also has a subway entrance, which sounds convenient, until you realize the stops are all actually a half-mile away at Times Square proper. So, even though there is a subway entrance right outside the hotel, you'll still need to spend 10-15 minutes walking underground to the train. With all of that being said, it's still better than walking above-ground and fighting your way through the crowd outside the Shake Shack or any other tourist trap along the way. The underground connector also shields you from the elements, which would be invaluable if it's cold/raining/snowing outside.

OK, it was somebody's bright idea to build a luxury hotel on 44th and 8th; I'm over it. I guess it's time to talk about the hotel itself. After walking through the chaos outside and retreating into what should be your sanctuary of a "luxury hotel", you are only greeted by more chaos. The revolving door is finicky; the elevators are finicky, the lobby's bizarre layout results in customers coming from three different directions, all intersecting at the exact point where the hallway narrows, and the luggage is left

A few months back we were disgusted to check into a room here that had urine stains all over the toilet and weeks worth of dust everywhere. Housecleaning manager promptly came up to investigate and admitted that they were having problems with this one employee and witnessed an uncomfortable exchange between them. Apologetic but nonetheless a failure on her part too, not to inspect room before giving OK? Cleaned up room and appeased our uneasiness with a complimentary breakfast...OK we move on.
However upon checking out of our room, my wife and I do a clean sweep. We check under the bed and could not believe what we saw and how we failed our child. I must have counted at least half a dozen nuts among other things. Now you see the problem here is... my son is severely anaphylactic to nuts. Having played with his cars during stay and one of them mistakingly gone under the bed's edge and reached to find it... a tragedy could of ensued by just touching one of them. We were furious and explained situation at front desk. Everyone concerned and shocked at my pictures that I had taken. Was told Hotel manager will contact you at home. Left very disappointed.
Here is where things get interesting. Hotel Manager calls and politely attempts to make amends as I divulge I have stayed here numerous times and given you rave reviews but this time around we were appalled. Perhaps attempting to avoid a bad review he makes a peace offering." Please give us another chance and here is my number and I will personally take great care of you next time around". I wondered how...considering my son was unknowingly a foot away near death during entire stay. " Don't worry just call me."
So here we are, I take him up on his offer recently. I do call and guess what? To my surprise he gives me a wrong number. OK. Nonetheless I track him down and call him at hotel and drop him a message... that was never returned. OK... I am still coming. Inquired daily during stay to let him know we were there. Told

Hotel offers a good location, good hardware but too much attitude. Other than doormen and bellmen, it is hard to get a smile from anyone. If I was on business, I wouldn’t even comment but when you are on family holiday, hospitality does make a difference. I understand It is a high season but there is no excuse of lacking in cleaning efforts, as I found a cork in the floor besides trash can and it wasn’t ours as we were just checked in. I asked for additional towels and they never got it right and not to mention cogfee restocking. This year I was quitting IHG for Marriott but had this hotel booked for a family holiday so did my stays to maintain status; now, I feel cheated as I haven’t even received a welcome note...

I never knew how much being a Platinum Starwood member makes a difference, until I travelled for the first time without one. I have stayed at dozens of starwood properties around the world, and it has always been great customer service, free couples massages, early check in, late check outs, etc. Gave no thought to how I would be treated as someone who "booked through a third-party travel website" --in this case Travelocity. Oh to experience how this hotel treats the regular folk was horrifying to say the least. So my friend and I get in around 1, so we think no problem let's drop off our bags, precheck in early, gave the front desk clerk my phone number to call me if the room is available earlier, and we go to lunch and come back at regular check in time--3pm. We come back at 10 minutes until 3, and now the lobby is packed. Check in time, right, did not think about that ever because like I said before always travelled with a platinum member on points so never had to wait until 3. It takes us 10 minutes to get to the front, she asks for my name and credit card again--i explain to her we already gave her my credit card etc. She checks on the room and says it is ready, but they are just checking to make sure it is inspected--no more than 10 minutes. 10 minutes go by, oh there's a bulb that needs to be replaced, 10 more minutes. 30 minutes go by, I am at this point agitated and very tired and the manager checks on the room and voila it's ready. So at this point it's 3:35, I am exhausted just want to lay down and check my phone which was in the front pocket of my backpack that i checked. I open up the pocket, and hooray the phone is cracked. When i last used it on the train, it was fine. Now it is cracked. I try to call down to the front desk, but the phone is broken in the room. So then I call them from my cell phone, and explain the situation and they tell me they will send an engineer to fix my phone and security to take down a report about my phone. Engineer shows u

Sometimes there is comfort in a nondescript hotel chain property, which can mean a reliable standard of service and upkeep. And sometimes if can mean a property that does not meet the expected standard. Our stay here was the latter, starting with check in. Early checkin was done online through Bonvoy app, which had no effect whatsoever. We were rudely told it never works and that there was no way in hell a 1 pm checkin would ever have been accommodated. Inasmuch as it was 2:50 pm at the time, we were told our room was still not ready and they would not give us another room that was ready because our booking was part of a block of rooms for a wedding no matter what my Binvoy status was. Nor was the room ready at 3 pm. It was closer to 3:30 pm. The room itself—1516–was pretty minuscule for a Sheraton branded property and felt almost like a windowless room as it just had the tiniest of windows ticked into the corner. The toilet seat was not firmly attached to the toilet and the motion sensing bathroom light turns on an incredibly loud fan if one walks even near the bathroom door, so that a trip to the bathroom in the middle of the night cannot be done without waking up one’s partner. Finally, the public men’s room on the second floor was positively revolting, with clogged and overflowing stall toilets in worse shape that the public restroom at Washington Square Park.

I never knew how much being a Platinum Starwood member makes a difference, until I travelled for the first time without one. I have stayed at dozens of starwood properties around the world, and it has always been great customer service, free couples massages, early check in, late check outs, etc. Gave no thought to how I would be treated as someone who "booked through a third-party travel website" --in this case Travelocity. Oh to experience how this hotel treats the regular folk was horrifying to say the least. So my friend and I get in around 1, so we think no problem let's drop off our bags, precheck in early, gave the front desk clerk my phone number to call me if the room is available earlier, and we go to lunch and come back at regular check in time--3pm. We come back at 10 minutes until 3, and now the lobby is packed. Check in time, right, did not think about that ever because like I said before always travelled with a platinum member on points so never had to wait until 3. It takes us 10 minutes to get to the front, she asks for my name and credit card again--i explain to her we already gave her my credit card etc. She checks on the room and says it is ready, but they are just checking to make sure it is inspected--no more than 10 minutes. 10 minutes go by, oh there's a bulb that needs to be replaced, 10 more minutes. 30 minutes go by, I am at this point agitated and very tired and the manager checks on the room and voila it's ready. So at this point it's 3:35, I am exhausted just want to lay down and check my phone which was in the front pocket of my backpack that i checked. I open up the pocket, and hooray the phone is cracked. When i last used it on the train, it was fine. Now it is cracked. I try to call down to the front desk, but the phone is broken in the room. So then I call them from my cell phone, and explain the situation and they tell me they will send an engineer to fix my phone and security to take down a report about my phone. Engineer shows u

Don't waste your money, it's overpriced and the rooms are trashed. Rooftop Bar is GREAT but other than that stay elsewhere, they need a complete overhaul and they are aware yet they advertise as if this is a 5 star, it's a 2 at best. I've stayed in 100's of hotels in NY and this is by far the worst. I actually could have stayed at the Ritz Carlton again for what they charges here. So disappointed and basically ruined my Birthday weekend.

Well, it looks like the Conrad NY has regressed. After staying here several times and being quite content I am here now and its very disappointing. I used the Conrad app that I always do to request 2 things. Xtra pillows and a room w a pullout sofa. I dont think thats too much or too difficult to accomodate, do you. I never received a call from the hotel which is normal procedure when you use the app. So I called and requested the same things. At check-in I confirmed the requests which were being accommodated( thats 3 times if your keeping track) Check in was long again 3 clerks for 14 people. Fast forward to evening and low and behold no xtra pillows and no sheets. My own fault I shouldve checked earlier but seriously, 3 requests and 2 confirmations and still nothing. So I call and tell them what I'm telling you and they said sorry well send it up. Guess what, 20 min later and nothing still. I cancel my reservations for 11 because Im not gonna go out and call the front desk at 3 am for pillows and sheets. This actually isnt the first time the Conrad has done this either. I call again and ask whats the issue and another sorry and 10 min later it finally arrives. To make a long story even longer I'm a Hilton Honors member and have stayed here more than 10 times starting when they were Embassy suites to now. The Conrad has been taking small steps backward over my last couple of stays and this stay is the worst to date. Maybe Im overreacting but that kind of simple request thats ignored really annoys me and the Conrad NY is officially off my favorites list.

This was our first time at this hotel The reviews that we read were encouraging for a girls weekend. The front desk was not welcoming and were actually rather rude. We upgraded the room to a deluxe, but it wasn't that much larger for the cost. But, that's not all. We arrived back that night at 10:30 to an uncleaned room, dirty towels and unmade beds. That was the first time that we had ever experienced this kind of thing. We called the front desk and they sent someone up. We were tired and it was late, but we left so that she could (Yvonne) clean the room. The front desk sent us up tickets for breakfast the next morning, which I would have never bought for the price. What a way to relax. Being silver Hilton Rewards members, I really thought the service would be better than we received.

We stayed here just recently on a stay to New York and the best part of the stay was the view from the hotel room. When we arrived we stopped by the concierge desk to inquire about a boat tour and was sold 4 adult tickets instead of the 2 adult 2 child tickets requested and would not have known this if it were not for the honesty of the boat company when we arrived at the box office. Plus depending on the concierge questioned they charged different rates for the same tickets.

Prior to arriving I checked in online and requested 2 extra towels and 2 foam pillows, these were not in the room and it was a bit late to be trying to fix this when we arrived back from out night out at 12 AM. When we showered and were attempting to dry off one of the towels had an offensive odor to it.

When we called the 45 minutes prior to departure for the Valet to get our car as asked to do I spent 15 minutes trying to reach the hotel operator, front desk, and valet by room phone and cell phone without luck. It rang and rang. I ended up going to the lobby where I informed them of these things and there was nothing that they could do other than to check me out and tell me that they would have a manager call me back. The resolve was not appropriate for what we put up with. I would like to say that this is where it ended but it is not.

My daughter left her brand new sweatshirt that she bought with her spending money the night before from One world observatory on the on the end of the bed at check out accidentally. When we realized this we tried calling and were told to call back as the Security staff was having to respond to something. I did as instructed and was told they were still busy and they would have someone call me back, hours later this still had not happened and I called and was told they did not know why I had not been called back. Then after repeating my name and room number many times the guy glanced at the log and said nope it was not turned in. I explained the frustration a

Just checked out. They charged me full rate, though the 3rd night had a promo. Impossible to reach the front desk as they don’t pick up the phone. The lounge is boring, same exact thing every morning and evening. What a great customer service for a diamond member.

I couldn't sleep 2 nigths because of the loud music in the rooftop bar. They changed me to a higher floor and I was comfortable the rest of my stay. But, they knew that floor was noisy so they should have even seal the windows or close those rooms. The rate was more than 400 dollars a night, so I was very angry. You can get much better hotels in NY.

I've stayed at this hotel for 5 years when I'm in town for business. Staff is great, the espresso machine is phenomenal, and I love the filtered water station on each floor. However, when I stayed there this February I left with terrible bug bites on my hands and lower back (where pajama pants and shirt meet). My hands swelled up and were unbearably itchy, hot and painful for over a week. It wasn't bedbugs, but something was eating me. I am bummed because this is the best hotel for the price point in FiDi, but I will be steering clear for quite some time :( I know you guys read these reviews: Please fix this problem! I was in pain and crazy itchy for a while.

We have chosen this hotel,after 25 visits in New York,because of the reviews written in Tripadvisor,for celebrating our special 30th wedding anniversary.Unfortunately by arrival we discovered that nothing of the things we had asked for, could come true.We have booked one bedroom suite with upgrade to a terrace suite,as we wanted space and opening door.They assured us that this will be done 2 days later,but cheating us in a cruel way, they never have done this upgrade that have promised.Apart from this,in the suite we stayed we had a lot of problems:very noisy A/C in the bedroom and we did not sleep well the whole stay( 10 nights),the curtain did not open,although we asked to fix it 3 times,so we had not enough light in,one telephone was out of use,the entire suite had no paper or pen to write,.the furniture were old and the advantages of choosing many pillows to choose never appeared.The location is very good indeed,but in the same neighborhood,there exist many choices in the same or better category.Τhe reception stuff was very typical but not helpful
We would never choose this hotel for our next visit to New York,for sure

Tiny room, terribly hot and uncomfortable and we couldn’t adjust the AC. My husband could barely shower it was so small! We had to wait a long time for elevators and breakfast was terrible! We also thought we were getting floor to ceiling windows but maybe that was at Hyatt House instead of Place.

The handle for the towel was broken , the freezer was not cooling nothing, when you turn the hot water make a huge pipeline noise, elevator always have one in maintenance even in heavy traffic time, the usb charger was not working as well! Shame of hotel to be honest! Breakfast is terrible, always the same, no variety of food! I stay for 5 days and just stand the breakfast for 2 days! Terrible! Elevator always busy i need use the stairs and also meet many people on the stairs in breakfast time and working time rush hours! Expensive and poor conditions hotel for a Hyatt name! Really my first bad experience at a Hyatt hotel!

There are too few elevators. Takes forever to reach the room.
Lobby is very dirty
Staff was overwhelmed and unable to help guests
The breakfast room is too small to accommodate every guest. Very dirty. Too few staff taking care of breakfast room
Very small room
Extremely expensive

We are very disappointed in our stay, the only good thing about this hotel is the location. The rooms are ridiculously small and lack some of the amenities indicated when booking. The door to the bathroom doesn't properly close, so be prepared to listen to everything your roommate is doing in there :/ The breakfast had very poor choice of food, really disappointing.

One of the worst hotels I’ve been to.
The price is very high and the service is one of the poorest I have ever experienced.
The rooms have no amenities at all. Nothing in the bathroom.
The front desk is not helpful and they don’t care about clients at all.

Had a major security issue while staying at this hotel. Was awakened at 1 AM to the noise of someone trying to enter my hotel room. The person repeatedly tried to open the door and only stopped when I yelled out in fear. The front desk informed me that a glitch in their computer was responsible for the error in room assignment (the person trying to enter my room was mistakenly assigned to the same room as me). The hotel manager later apologized in an email for the “inconvenience” and offered me a complimentary breakfast for their error. I wouldn’t consider this an inconvenience, rather a major security issue and something that should never occur in any reputable hotel. Thankfully I had the deadbolt locked, otherwise that person would have likely been able to enter my room in the middle of the night

I am staying at the hotel, and I am a bit frustrated. Small room, bathroom gets soaked as the shower glass is not well installed on the floor. I asked for an extra pillow, and nobody responds. The room has no minibar for cans and bottles… it was mentioned in the room description when we booked.

I stayed frequently at the Millennium Hilton pre 9/11 when on Business. Since then I have only stayed once, a number of years back, and was put off by the noise of the construction. As I was in New York again on Business, and construction has move on so greatly on the WTC site, I decided to try again. I have to say how disappointed I was with the overall experience and won’t be staying again.

I tend to visit New York at least once a year and stay at Soho Grand, Andaz Wall Street, Club Quarters etc. There is a gulf of difference between the level of service these hotels offer and the Millennium Hilton, but not a gulf in cost. The only bright spot was being able to check in at 12am but, apart from that, the service at the Front Desk was brusquely efficient at best.

I was given a room on the 9th Floor overlooking the construction with a wonderful view of a crane immediately opposite my window. As far as noise was concerned it was surprisingly quieter than I thought but on the 3rd night I was awake at 5am and eventually just gave up and left for the office. Although clean, the room was small, tired and stuffy despite it being mid Nov. After trying to adjust the heat controls I gave up and just turned off the Air Con/Heating unit. As I was on business I did not spend too much time in the room, which was a blessing.

Despite being a Hilton Honors member, there was no little extra’s that other establishments offer such as complimentary water (so on the 1st morning I was forced to the pricey mini bar) and newspapers. All in all the whole experience was completely impersonal and unwelcoming.
The hotel is in a great downtown location (if you can accept the ongoing construction work opposite) being close to the Financial District, Tribeca etc. However, there are many other hotels with far more reasonable rates that are just as convient but where the focus on service is poles apart.

Website was misleading. Shows and states 'restaurant on site'...."room service"....nope this is closed...we totally understand due to covid...but put that front and center on the site. Not in the 'about covid section'!

Rooms are shabby, nasty stain on the chair...seriously gross...see pictures. Lamp is not working...asked to repair replace...5 hours later nothing was done. Furniture beat up and old...no blankets?? Rug is stained and ripped.trim and molding is in need of repair. Not coming back at this rate...to be honest I dont think they want us here.

As an IHG Diamond Ambassador, very unhappy with this stay. I had lowered my expectations based on previous reviews but hoped for the best as the location was convenient to where I needed to be. The woman at check-in was pleasant. Breakfast is served until 9:30, so if you’re a late riser, be aware. The television was not working, nor the remote control but we we didn’t want to bother waiting fir a technician. The room faced the street which was noisy with sirens, garbage trucks, etc. Inside the room, the mini fridge kept making a noise every 15 minutes as did the heating unit. The room and bathroom were tiny, making it impossible to have two people in the bathroom at the same time. The room felt old and dingy. Although there is a quiet policy in place from 11:00 pm - 7:00 am. We heard every single noise from occupants next door as well as building personnel early in the morning. The comforter was quite thin as well. A person hopes to get a good night’s sleep in a hotel. Unfortunately, this was not the case. Bring earplugs and ask for a room facing the back.

Hate to rate this hotel poorly but it's due to the noise outside. Front desk staff was outstanding. Very friendly, smiles in their faces and very helpful. The room was nice & clean. So from first appearances all the way through going to sleep, this hotel would get 5 stars. Problems happen around 5 AM. I was in the 9th Floor and faced the street. This was not high enough to not hear the extremely loud garbage truck out front. If the track just passed it would be one thing but it seemed to stay parked forever in front of the building. By the time it finally leaves the noises start at the construction site next door. Very loud with an extremely annoying tapping notice that just doesn't stop. So much for sleeping in a little :(. After working a very long day, having a corporate after hours event you look toward to a great night sleep. This did not happen here

I know there are only certain things in your control and know there is not much you can do here but maybe there should be a notice on your website about the very large loud construction site next door. Sorry guys but if you can't get a good night rest there is no way I can rate higher.

If this was a two-star hotel I would be impressed, but it is certainly not a four-star hotel. We made a reservation almost six months in advance for a King room on a high floor. They first gave us a King room on a low floor...where the 48th street traffic is loud. When we asked to be changed they first told us no problem that there were King rooms on the 10 and 12th floor, but we had to wait a day. As we are staying for 10 days that was not a big problem. However, the next day they could only offer a twin room on the 10th floor...with a window next to a construction site! It is impossible to sleep and we my wife and I can't even sleep together in the same bed. This has been one of the worst experiences I have ever had. One hotel staff said it was due to the UN General Assembly being in town...I have come to the UNGA for thirty years and even stayed at this hotel in the past for of the two week session, but I have never been treated so poorly before. I am especially annoyed because we recommended this hotel to about two dozen other partners who are staying here...and complaining to us. It was a mistake to have stayed here and one we won't make in the future.

First of all, I am a gold elite member and I have stayed at this hotel multiple times over the last several months, almost every month for several months. I stayed at this hotel from July 4th until July 19TH, 2018, in room 1436, and woke up with a few spots of skin irritation on my face. Then every day, these "irritations" popped up more and more and I didn't suspect it was a bug bite because it didn't itch and it honestly just looked like a minor skin irritation and that's why I did not report it to the hotel. I have seven of those spots and now after 4-5 days later, they started to itch like crazy, and it;s been a week now and they are definitely some kind of bites. I had a friend who stayed with me, and she also has same spots that have been itching like crazy. Google image search show they are unmistakably brown recluse spider bites but what makes me MAD is I've emailed the hotel and marriott twice about this and I still have not gotten a response from them. When I finally did get a call, the person who was "investigating" was unpleasant, insinuated that that I had the bites before I came to the hotel. This has been bothering me very much to say the last, I feel feverish from the bites and they look nasty, not to mention the itch that doesn't seem to be helped with any cortisone cream. I just find this unacceptable, I knew the hotel didn't have a great service, but this is supposedly a "historic, landmark" marriott hotel, and the fact that no one has reached out to me after repeated contact with them. - Addendum: The bites are mostly all healed now but they left me lovely scars. If you decide to stay at this hotel, REPORT AND COMPLAIN EVERY LITTLE SKIN IRRITATION YOU WAKE UP WITH and demand to get a different room, document, take pictures and demand that they compensate you. It's been month, I've gotten a letter asking to investigate it further, I've replied to the email in the letter and still NOTHING.

This wasn't my first stay at Affinia 50. I always liked the hotel. They have suites with nice kitchens and a reasonable cost-benefit. But they have an issue with air conditioning. We arrived at last april 17. The weather was sunny and hot, about 28 centigrades. But, inside it was hotter. But the air conditioning only worked in heat function.
When I complain they sad that the air conditioning system was not working cause of the season. They sad that in winter the air conditioning only works in heat mode, in summer in cold mode and between seasons it does not work anyway.
I said to then that that was an hotel and not a school or a prison where someone in charge rules above others. People has different needs and that is the point of you have an individual control of air conditioning in each room.
The staff tried to help and they moved us to the affinia gardens(where they have the same problem, but the person in charge changed the air conditioning system to cool).
I don't think that a hotel that claims to have "air conditioning" could work this way.
So, you don't like to sweat, Affinia, only on summer...

I traveled to New York last month.
I made the reservation to this hotel 3 months ago but when I arrived at the hotel an unpleasant surprise just waiting for me ! The room that I had reserved was not available ! The reservation was for a king room with view in Times Square which was an extra charge in room's price due to the view ! Though when I arrived at the hotel, the room wasn't available. When I made the reservation I had informed the hotel that I will realize a late check in ! Unfortunately a young lady at the reception desk just anounced to me that the room was any longer available , and a colleague of hers instead of apologizing to me for their mistake his first reaction was if I had tried to contact with the hotel ! I answered to him that I emailed to them twice and besides through the site that I had made the reservation was already specified to them ! The young lady had gone to ask her manager about the mistake but when she returned I heard her colleague whispering to her that it was my mistake ! He was rude ! I received a corner similar to the price of the room with the view but nothing in common considering the view which totally disappointing ! Besides the access in the hotel was difficult to be found due to renovations which wasn't clear in the hotel directions. Accidentally we managed to find our way to the hotel after going in circles about an hour. The same thing happened to us the day after and when we was totally lost we got a police officer offered to help us by giving them a call to ask for directions. Unfortunately there was no reply ! So we wee totally disillusioned ! The breakfast was very expensive and totally lost of money, too gravy and lacked in diversity ! Make sure that breakfast is not included to the room price !
I am really sorry because it is a 4 star hotel but during my stay there I only confront rudeness and through the receptionists and the portal men as well. The reason that I am saying that is because we used the valet parking

I plan to stay at this hotel in the coming March by splitting into 2 periods. First, I will stay on 17th March for one night and come back again on 21st March for the other 2 nights.

I would like to ask them whether they allow me to leave my suitcase there (between the 2 periods) or not. and also any charge for this service.

I tried all means to contact with them including
1) sent them and inquiry email BUT NO ANSWER
2) sent them a personal message on Facebook page, they READ BUT NO ANSWER.
3) sent an inquiry through agoda.com, NO REPLY
Finally
4) I decided to make an international call to the hotel number +1-212-324-3774. I have to wait approximately 3 minutes before I can talk to the "real staff" then he took about 4 minutes before "understanding my question". I do not think my English skills is not that bad for explaining such a simple question. And then, he let me wait "FOREVER" for getting the answer. As you know, the cost of making an international call is not cheap. I could even buy BigMac with up-size fries and Coke for my whole family, honestly! I totally give up, can't bear holding on the line any longer.

Many reviews honored their mesmerizing and breathtaking views. I also would like to stay here as they claim that this place is the tallest hotel in North America. But, you know, if your hotel is the tallest, your service is at the rock bottom.

I am Hilton Honors Diamond. I have a family member undergoing potential Cancer surgery. I am in town to go with them for medical consultation. To ease the anxiety I thought I would stay at a full service hotel. This hotel is not full service. I was mistaken.

The check in on Friday required a 20 minute wait in both the Hilton Honors and regular line.

When I needed a pair of pants laundered I was told that was impossible on a Saturday. When I complained and asked to speak to a manager, the manager informed me no New York Hotel does laundry on Saturdays and if I needed my pants cleaned there are laundromats and I could go to one.

Having stayed in New York before at other hotels I knew this was untrue. When I unfortunately lost my patience he told me I shouldn’t be upset with him.

I then get a call an hour later from the front office manager who tells me that they know that specific manager has issues - and if I bring my pants back before 11:30 pm they will launder them.

When I return at approximately 9:30 pm they know nothing. So I yell and complain as they did launder my pants.

So I decided to get a drink in the bar on Sunday. I ordered a Manhattan cocktail which came with a dead fly floating in it. They replaced it but the bartender told me they have a problem with flies in the bar.

If you are Hilton Honors don’t expect much from the Executive Lounge here. There is NO hot breakfast here - only hard boiled eggs and danish and fruit. Funny other Hilton’s give you a full breakfast. Hampton Inn gives you more. As a Diamond member you don’t get hot breakfast here unless you complain.

It’s Sunday night and when I walked into the Executive Lounge at 8 pm there was NO food - just bottled water and coffee.

Let me clarify a few things. I am paying for my current stay. Second, i have stayed here twice before with no issues. Given what I had experienced in the past makes this stay extremely disappointing to say the least.

After staying here I feel that my Diamond statu

This was my fifth and nearly my final stay at the Mela. Up until now could not fault it for value, location and its amenities.

On this stay on arrival I reported a fault as the inclusive wifi was not working in the room. I was told it was a service provider fault impacting all businesses that would be corrected within 2 hours.

Next day still not working. Now advised it is a hotel fault and an engineer was looking into it.

Without boring you with the whole story - 5 nights later, my stay is over and it is still broken with no end in sight. As a consequence, instead of enjoying my room, I spent most of my time before and after work in our offices. I could not contact my family and if not for Starbucks I would have been totally cut off. And as for my roaming bill for work - it is going to be astronomical!!

I did visit the front desk and asked who I should complain to. I was given a named business card and sent a note on Wednesday. With no response, I contacted front desk to find out they'd given me a card for a manager who was out for a few days!!!

Luckily I was speaking to a manager when I found this out and he swiftly looked into these issues and resolved it with an apology (first one in 5 days) and reduced the rate by $30 per night. If not for his action I would not be choosing them again. (Due to come back in a few weeks).

Any way, on this stay, wanted to say that the room I was given was the best yet in terms of size and space. Unfortunately this detail did get lost in the wifi issues which to most modern travellers is key.

I don't understand how people rate this hotel is so highly. Have traveled a lot for work / pleasure and have always found the trip advisor reviews to be fairly accurate. Not in this case.

Have never had a hotel room this small. I should post a picture as the room really looks like a closet with a bed stuck in. On a positive room was clean and staff was friendly but there are much nicer hotels in the are for the same price. Give it a 1 because this is supposed to be a 4 star hotel and it clearly is no where close.

Aside from the room there is nothing special about this hotel (other than the attached restaurant). Hotel in general is small and drab with little amenities.

Take heed of every review you read here about the tiny rooms – it’s absolutely true.

We spent four nights here earlier in the month. After a 7-hour flight to New York and 2 hours negotiating New York’s public transport system, we arrived at 2 p.m. needing a rest. The hotel knew where we were travelling from but still didn’t think it was important that there was a room waiting for us when we got there. We tried to check in at 2 only to be told that they were allocating a larger room to us but that we’d have to wait an hour (okay, check-in is given as 3 – but why? Checkout is at noon. What are they doing for three hours?).

Everyone else in the queue with us (with only one person on reception handling us) were getting their rooms.

Well, if this was a ‘larger room’, I can’t imagine how tiny the other room would have been. I couldn’t quite believe it. Why they thought a couple travelling from Ireland for four nights would require an office desk and chair is beyond me. We didn’t use it once and it took up pretty much all the space.

The bed was smaller than we have at home, but on the plus side it was comfortable. Housekeeping was excellent.

The bathroom was small but okay. The shower/bath was very shallow and positioned so close to the toilet that I hurt my foot on it stepping in.

The only good lighting was in the bathroom. The room was small, poky and dark, with no view except into other people’s rooms or the sides of boring buildings so high you could hardly see the sky. You couldn’t walk past the wardrobe when the doors were open, that’s how tight the space was. There was only room for one locker by the side of the bed as the distance between the bed and the radiator/aircon the other side was hardly enough to squeeze through.

The décor was drab and mismatched (chocolate chair, lime green cushion and duck egg blue curtains that didn't close? Please!). The carpets were a horrible brown colour with tacky 1970s squares. If you were susceptible to migraines it wouldn

I always loved the spaciousness of the rooms, but they are getting in desperate need of renovation. The beds are comfortable, but the rooms seem worn out and dark.

Housekeeping just straightened up the room one day in what seemed to be very rushed. They forgot to bring the towels!

The front desk was not the most welcoming and did not present the coupons for my American Express amenities. They were put under my door two days later with no note or explanation. I did bring it to the front desk manager's attention.

As for the restaurant, Gordon Ramsey needs to do his own Kitchen Nightmares in this establishment. Go to the coffee shop around the corner. Much less expensive and much better quality!

This is the first ever one star review I've given -- and I've stayed at hundreds of hotels around the world. We'd stayed here many times previously, so we were willing to overlook a disappointing experience on our prior stay to give them another try, even though we'd heard that the hotel had been steadily declining under new management. Big mistake. We'd reserved a suite with a king-size bed for my daughter and me to share, but what we got was a full-size, not even a queen. The room was shabby as well. It took several calls to get hold of anyone at the front desk, and once we did we were put on an interminable hold. When we finally got through to someone he flatly told us that the hotel was fully booked and our only other option would be a smoking room. The worst kept secret in the industry is that every large hotel keeps rooms available for unforeseen circumstances, so he obviously took us for idiots. Meanwhile, our booking agent, although trying numerous times, couldn't get anyone at the London to even answer his call. Once he finally reached someone and exerted some pressure they caved and gave us the room that we were supposed to have in the first place -- which miraculously became available. All of this drama took more than two hours, making it too late for us to go out to eat. When we told this to the manager we assumed that he would offer us room service or some kind of accommodation. We were wrong. He did, after our complaining long enough and loud enough, offer us a $100 discount on our room. We won't stay here again, and strongly recommend that anyone else considering this hotel look elsewhere.

Rooms dark and pretentious - want to be modern but are dated. Public area on first blush looks OK but really not functional. Breakfast seating frankly uncomfortable but food quite good. We had a security problem and staff were frankly unhelpful until we made a fuss. This is a hotel that made me feel entirely underwhelmed and overcharged...be careful of their cancellation policy!

Stayed here with a friend for 2 nights in order to go to the Metropolitan Opera. The location was PERFECT. Literally across the street from the Met.

Unfortunately, I have little else good to say about The Empire Hotel.

My friend had stayed there once before on a business trip, and came away with a favorable impression, which is why we decided to stay there.

The good: The doorman was very nice, and the front desk staff were pleasant and competent.
The bad:
--the room was musty-smelling
--the room was in poor condition with wall paper that was starting to peel and missing trim pieces
--the climate control offered very little control over the temperature of the room, which was too warm and stuffy
--our room was an interior room, i.e. no view, which I would have normally not cared about, but it was in the first floor above ground level overlooking a bunch of noisy, ugly equipment so we had to keep the shades closed all the time.
--the room was dark and gloomy because we had to keep the shades closed
--the wifi cost extra and was slower than molasses. I shelled out $19.99/day and gave up even trying to get my email to download.
--the elevators were slow when they worked, but often simply never came when called.
--there was a coffee pot in the room, but each coffee pod was $3.00.

I really dislike it when I stay at an expensive hotel and they charge for things like coffee and Internet, which every moderately-priced hotel includes for free.

So very disappointing.

I recently stayed at The Empire Hotel while I was running a training nearby. If you’ve gone to Lincoln Center before, you’ve walked by this hotel! It’s a great location, very easy to get to from a lot of things. The experience I’ll detail below is atypical and much of what it was out of control of the hotel’s staff. There was an explosion/fire in the basement that caused power to go out and alarms to go off.

I’ll first comment on my general experience outside of the incident. The hallways had a funky vibe with an interesting picture frame wallpaper. The room was nice, it was mostly well designed with a decent amount of space for a hotel in NYC. I had one queen size bed, with a work desk and a bench to put your suitcase. There was a mini bar and a tray of goodies on the desk, however there was no pricelist to be found! The fridge was stocked pretty full, but you had a slight bit of room to add your own bottle. The bathroom looked recently redone and offered a nice bit of storage. The shower had been redone with a rain shower, however it wasn’t well designed. It looked as if it was originally a stand-up shower with a sliding door, however the part of the door that slides was taken out, so it was partially open. The shower wasn’t that large and each time I showered I ended up with a pool of water on the bathroom floor! It was a nice look and concept, but unfortunately not well executed. The bed was comfortable and the room was clean. As I always mention, if you’re not used to staying in a big city/NYC – be sure to set your expectations with the size of the room. Everything is small – don’t expect tons of space – your room is probably bigger than some apartments in NYC! … but then again you’re not in NYC to camp out in your room.

Now I’ll comment on where things didn’t go well. When checking in I wanted to confirm that the room was being paid for by the training organizer and would not go on my card.  The person at the front desk had to ask around, but told me the car

I can't lie - I really didn't enjoy my stay. It wasn't terrible, but it really missed the mark for me. First, when we got there, I noticed a door connecting to another room. It was unlocked on our side. Yes, of course I locked it, but if I hadn't been paying attention, I would have gone to sleep with the potential for the person in the next room to walk right in. Maybe that seems petty, but creepy things are known to happen now and then. I expect my hotel to look out for my safety. To have the staff leave the room vulnerable that way was careless. We got an accessible room which we didn't request, but that usually doesn't present any problem for me. This room had a bathroom that I really can't imagine working well for someone using a wheelchair. There was no place to put anything (which was also true in my friend's regular bathroom.) The sink was so shallow that if you turned it on with anything but the lowest force, it splashed all over the place. There was no towel for the floor (I thought for wheelchair safety, but my friend's regular bathroom didn't have a floor mat either,) so we had to use one of our two hand towels for that. In the main room, there were three problems if you don't count the lumps in the floor: The smoke detector had a blinking light that was quite annoying when the room was dark and we wanted to sleep; the sockets next to the bed where I plugged in my phone to charge turn off when you turn off the lights behind the bed. Of course my battery was dead when I woke up. Whose dumb idea was that? And one of my worst pet peeves of hotel beds happened: When I woke up, the bottom sheet was completely untucked and the I was partly sleeping on the mattress pad - Ewww. If you can't use a fitted sheet, then at least get one large enough to tuck in far enough to stay there. The look of the room was nice enough, but there was nothing that felt convenient. Well, to be fair, at least there was a room safe. In addition to Service, Sleep Quality and Location, I

The Paul NYC is a terrible hotel. The hotel claims to be non-smoking but when we checked in we found that the room we were assigned to had a strong cigarette smell that almost knocked us out. We then had to scramble late at night to find a new hotel to stay at since there were no other rooms available. The front desk employee assured as that we would most likely get a refund as this was not our fault. I have severely sensitive to the cigarette smell and there was no ways a bit of air freshener would have solved the problem. We did ask to switch to another room but unfortunately there was none available and the front desk said there was nothing else they could do. To this day, the Paul NYC has been refusing to get back to us on the refund. They also have been continuously claiming that the manager is out of reach. On top of our issues, the hotel seems to also have other maintenance issues as we overheard other guests complaining about incorrect room assignment and broken AC. I have not been able to get in touch with anyone via the phone at the hotel. Apparently the hotel phone number is just for show and would only lead you to a full voice mail no matter what option you choose. Judging from the other reviews, it seems like there were a lot of similar complaints. I would suggest all other travelers to avoid staying here. This place is a scam.

I stayed at the Hampton Inn Manhattan Times Square central on Aug 29. The room is an ok room, nothing special about it. They give a complimentary hot breakfast, dining room was a bit crowded.
As many did point out the elevators during peak hours are slow, people said they walked up 20 floors rather than wait.
My biggest disappointment was when I tried to book the hotel for a future stay, the website and the people I spoke with were not knowledgeable, I was transferred around between the same people multiple times. I ended with no satisfaction. The lack of customer service (spending almost 2 hours on calls or internet) makes it where I do not know if I will ever stay at a Hampton Inn or Hilton again, the experience was that bad.

This is a story of outrageous treatment and poor follow-up customer service.
I booked this hotel because it is a short walk to the venue where we were to attend a very important family event the night of Dec 8th. Our flight was delayed coming in but we arrived at the hotel in time to get to our room, freshen up, change our clothes and take the short walk to the event. It was pretty cold so we were glad to get inside to check-in. I gave our name to the desk receptionist and he immediately told us that unfortunately the room being held for us had a plumping problem. I thought he was going to tell us they were working on it or that he was going to upgrade us to another type room. But no!!! He told us he had booked us a room at the Chelsea Holiday Inn and would pay for a cab to get us there. He handed me $10 and had me sign a voucher for it and that was it--not even goodbye!
It took almost 10 minutes for the doorman to hail an empty cab as we stood on the sidewalk in the freezing cold with our bags. We were literally dazed and confused as to what was happening and worried we weren't going to make the event on time.
Did I mention it was a Saturday night and the night of SantaCon???????
It took more than 20 minutes to crawl the 11/2 miles to the Holiday Inn and cost $15+tip.
The Holiday Inn is pretty shabby yet they charged us close to the same rate as the Washington Square Hotel and they aren't within walking distance to the venue we needed to get to. This is not a hotel we would have chosen to stay in and couldn't believe the Washington Square Hotel thought this was the right thing to do to a guest.
We then had to Uber through SantaCon crowds and Saturday night traffic again to head back to the venue which was less than a block from the Washington Square Hotel, where we had started! We were late.
