This great bastion of NYC hotel history is a pale shadow of its former self. From a reservations staff that tries to change room on you (watch out for "upgrades") and a hopeless system of check-in (where you send your ID period to check in, so that they lose it immediately, and they treat you like a complete stranger every time you approach the desk) to glacial room service, broken elevators, malfunctioning keys, the days of the Carlyle as "home away from home" are clearly over. Little things like the elimination of complementary coffee—I assume this was done as an expedience during COVID but restored in other hotels—and the virtual boarding up of the area that used to have the coffee (giving the entrance hall a shabby look), makes the huge cost of the rooms feel like being cheated. "Now the coffee's in the dining room" (which means an endless wait). The justly popular bar at the Carlyle has, for people staying in the hotel, the disadvantage not just of passing one's way through the crowds, but of being treated as if one is cutting the line by Carlyle staff, who bark at you as you emerge from the lobby. The side door is now closed during the day, again giving the feel that things are going down hill. And in all of this, there seems to be no coordination among staff: reservations are forgotten, luggage misplaced, room service arrives hours late, having been left somewhere to cool down.

Stayed here for the first time Dec 4-6, although others in the group have stayed here before. This was a bus trip, that has stayed here a number of times. The hotel was very clean, a convenient location and really have no complaints, except for the SAFETY ISSUES. In the morning, we were going to meet for breakfast when others in our group could not get out of their room. The door was literally locked! They were trapped inside! When they called the front desk, they were informed to call 911. Someone from the hotel did come to the room, and in about 30 minutes they got out. But, the frame had to be chiseled out. Very upset, when they informed the manager at the front desk if there was a fire, they could have been burned to death, he laughed at them. It took them from 8:30 Saturday morning until 5:00 pm to get another room. Totally unacceptable.

we have stayed here twice ( used Groupon both times) and both times were were disappointed by the room. the bathrooms are in desperate need of renovation. the carpet in the hallway is ancient and the room didnt smell fresh when we stayed the 2nd time. The location cant be beat if you need something in the times square area but dont expect much more than a bed to sleep in. The sheets were clean ( I always pull apart the bed to look!) and the mattress was comfy. our view was of the alley and the air conditioner was the loudest I have ever heard.

I have stayed at this property several times now, and it has gone downhill each time. The rooms themselves need updating and repairs (see photos). The service this time was particularly poor: at checkin they put a phantom $12.79 charge on my account for food purchased by somebody else hours earlier. When I questioned the odd credit card authorization I was told that it was for incidentals.

Then, getting it fixed on checkout, the clerk was seriously snippy about the whole thing. And of course there is the insulting $3 per bag storage fee if you have the gall to want to walk around for an hour or two after checking out and before getting a train.

There are other, better choices in this neighborhood now. Especially for the price.

I am a heavy traveler stay more than 200 nights in a hotel every year around the world, of which a big contribution goes to Hyatt chain. I arrived Grand Hyatt New York on 24 January 2012 and am still here today, checking out tomorrow 29 January 2012.

I did not know a 2 star hotel could be named Grand Hyatt.

I choose Grand Hyatt New York because of my trust to Grand Hyatt brand and I felt betrayed. Took me, as a high ranked Hyatt Goldpassport VIP 20 minutes to line up for check-in, at 21:00 pm, which I find difficult to understand why was there so few staff at front desk.

I have requested for 1 King size bed as my usual preference, and was told that was not available. But somehow "magically" a room because available upon request to talk to Duty Manager? The room was however at low floor that receives no sun light, I wonder how many Hyatt Goldpassport VIP was staying that had higher rank than me?

The water pressure was extremely low at shower, took twice or trice extra time to wash off soap and shampoo.

The location was good but given the fact any hotel located next to a subway station in New York would in fact share similar convinence, so can't really give that much credit.

Charging for 1-800 numbers and while asked for an explanation what should such be charged when Hyatt is not being charged, my phone was forwarded to front duck which did not pick up and put through to a voice messsage.

Barrowed an umbralla and was asked for room number and "reminded" to bring back otherwise will be charged for umbralla.

Very bad experience, will NEVER stay at this hotel again, and advice to those of you are considering, DON'T. And share to news to your caring ones so they don't have to go though this.

This is my first review at TripAdvisor, and the motivation is to help other travelers avoid bad experience.

To whom it may concern, Mold in my bathroom and a 2 hour manager response to handle the issue, after numerous calls. The mold issue was a serious concern for me and the party that I Invited for a Mother's Day Getaway. However the lack of disregard and respect that I received as a Guest, was unbelievable. Let me just start by saying that no room or hotel is perfect, nor do i expect them to be, the fact that I made reservation for 8 people in 2 rooms and we only received 3 sets of towels per room, no problem called down for extra towels and wash cloths. The shades would not go down, i called down and let somebody know, the gentleman then asked me did I see a blue light on the electric box inside the window I said no, he asked if I was able to stand on the desk and push the button on the electric box and reset the shade, I said I can try, I was able do as requested and the shade worked no problem. Safe did not work very nice man came up and replaced the batteries, no problem. Room was a bit on the dusty side , the sink in the bathroom did not drain well, again that was not a problem. However having mold in a room is a very big problem, especially when you do not notice it till the next day and you and your family have been breathing in mold spores all night and that can be deadly and can cause medical repercussions. So at that time I wanted a manager ASAP to come and resolve this issue. So I call housekeeping and I am told someone will be there within 15 minutes. 15 minutes goes by nothing, I call housekeeping again and I again am told somebody would get back to me in 15 min. 20 minutes go by and I call the operator and ask for the manager on Duty, I then get patched thru to a voicemail, I leave a message explaining my dilemma with the hope of getting a response. While waiting I again reach out to housekeeping to be told again 15 minutes. I wait another 20 minutes and call for the manager on duty only to again be sent to voicemail, where once again I leave a message. T

I had been here once before and thought it would be different.
It was not.
I liked the location.

Unfriendly, unattentive staff.
Valet parking 70 dollars a night. This is posted on the site by the way.
We stayed one night for a total of $848.68, yes we ordered coffee in room. No this was not a suite just a regular room.
Fridge, Phone, Bathroom filthy.
Bathroom stank as mold.
The wall paper on the side wall was bubbled.
The sink would clog up just by running water.
The fridge was dirty clearly not wiped or disinfected.
The heater did not work as the room was cold for my 2 kids 13 and 11 husband and myself.
The phone was filthy and dusty.
The hinged on the bottom of the door was coming off .
This is what I got for U$D 848.68
Very unsatisfied..
Unhappy..

DISAPPOINTED.

I was very happy to meet Sandra again who had promised me to add 12000 points to my account as I was treated very badly at check in, however till date I am still waiting for the points

She is one gem of lady who had helped me last time too.

The hotel has great location and ends there

I have stayed in this hotel several times in the past and last time I wrote a five star review for it, but unfortunately this time was a disappointment. I was travelling with my adult daughter on a family trip from Australia and wanted her first visit to NYC to be special. I told her what a great hotel this was and we booked and paid 7 months in advance. We had a 2 x DBL room with a Times Square view - $2168. for four nights. On arrival, the room we were given was not the standard I had in the past. It was small and dark with a number of problems like a blocked shower that wouldn't drain, a broken lock on the window so we couldn't close it, bathroom door wouldn't shut properly, broken cupboard hinges and general wear. I don't expect upgrades when I travel and have rarely ever had a problem with a room, but the next morning I spoke to Sandra at reception and asked if someone could fix the shower drain and the window lock. She immediately contacted Housekeeping and assured us it would be taken care of, but when we arrived back to our room in the evening, it was still the same. The window had now been opened, but we still could not shut it and stop the wind from rattling it. The next morning she was not at reception so we decided to give it another day - nothing happened and we were not contacted again. By the third day, we decided to try and forget the inconveniences and enjoy NYC.

On checkout, Jaime asked how we had enjoyed our stay. I asked him what we should book to get a nicer room next time, and he insisted that we should not have had those problems. He was going to get the manager to contact me. As I was returning to NYC three weeks later and staying at the Intercontinental, he was going to contact them to arrange something. He suggested I should have booked an Executive level room, however, there is nothing on the website by that name. In the past that is what I had booked, but this time if I booked a Deluxe room the hotel website specified that I would not be

Very disappointing communication from this hotel and IHG. 3 years now and still no refund after our cancellation due to covid. We were guaranteed our refund within 90 days then 120 days and so on, this was just so we wouldn't be in the time frame to request a credit card charge back. Shame on you crowneplaza and ihg group, you have done this to so many people.

Like I have seen on other reviews, had to cancel our trip booked for May 2020 and am still waiting for a refund of over £2000. We were told that we would have to wait until the hotel reopened but have heard nothing from them at all.

Not something I would have expected from Crowne Plaza, very disappointing.

[Fraud Billing, TWICE!]

We checked in on September 19. We planned to stay until September 24. Unfortunately my partner had an emergency issue on September 20 and had to stay in the hospital for a few days. So I came back on September 21 and asked if we could get the refund for the last two days. You checked with your manager and told me that we would get the refund for the prepaid on September 22 and 23 two nights.

On September 22 early in the morning I came to the reception for check-out. You printed a page of bill with USD$103.5. I fortunately looked at the bill and asked what it was for. You then looked on your screen and said it was nothing and made a new bill without any charge. THIS IS THE FIRST FRAUD BILLING.

This was the week of UN assembly. Can one imagine how many people who stayed here for UN but did not read the bill when they checked out?

On September 24 my partner was lucky to leave the ICU and came back to stay at your hotel for another two days. During our stay I checked on my AMEX records and found there was indeed USD$103.5 charge and refund on the same day, September 22. What surprised me was there was ANOTHER charge of USD$26 on September 21 by you, the day we were in the ER. THIS IS THE SECOND FRAUD BILLING.

For 45 minutes, 45 MINUTES, I explained and showed the USD$26 at your reception and you kept saying you did not see any charge.

I WAS AGGRESSIVE BECAUSE YOU DID NOT DO YOUR PROFESSIONAL JOB FOR 45 MINUTES.

Finally I got my USD$26 back because you found the charge in your computer system, after my 45 minutes loss. Shame on you!

FBI SHOULD PAY ATTENTION ON THESE, AND POSSIBLY MUCH MORE NOT FOUND, FRAUD BILLING BY INTERCONTINENTAL BARCLAY!

First off, I will say this is a very nice hotel in a great location. However, it seems to be managed quite poorly. We booked a 'Deluxe Double' room (2 beds) months in advance, but upon checking-in, we were told that they had 'run out' of doubles and would have to place us in a room with only one bed (despite our reservation). This was unfortunately a big deal since I was traveling with someone with health issues that made sleeping in the same bed difficult. The only compensation they provided for their mistake was free wifi.

This isn't the first time that this has happened with an IHG hotel. For context, I have platinum status with IHG and stay with them pretty frequently for both business and leisure. For me, this is a case of 'straw that broke the camel's back' and in the future, I will be giving my business to their competitors.

Couple of weeks before arriving i e mailed the hotel,8 days later no response, i contacted IHG twitter & ambassador desk to help me reach the hotel,i was promissed a response within 48 hours and still nothing,it gives you an idea before arriving the level of customer service.
On arrival i enquire about a parcel delivered and no one seems to know where it is.
Checking in at 10pm i expect my room to be thoroughly inspected,my disgust to find left over food from previous guest in the fridge,i will attach a pic.
1 espresso capsule was used and left in machine.
Ice bucket with no ice but was still wet like if someone hurrily try to dry it.
I wonder how long before my arrival this room was cleaned.
The manager attended my room to witness and promissed 20.000 points as an apology and these points never arrived,not that i am in need,i have over a million points sitting unused in my account but its the principle.
Next day at 6am the alarm set by previous guest went on ,in the darkness of the room i couldnt manage to disactivate it but to unplug the power socket and hitting my head and dislodging part of the bedside light.
Again enquiring about my parcel and no sign,showed them delivery proof and signature but no one seems to have a clue.
On my return to the room i realised they removed the fruit plate,removed the used soap with no replacement.
As far as the club lounge is concerned,no hot food a la carte to order on breakfast (unlike most IC club lounge around the world),soft dring are sealed in the fridge till 5 pm.
I cant understand the low standard Intercontinental have in the US,i am a Royal ambassador member and i hate staying at any intercontinental in North America,they have no sense of luxury,no appreciation of your loyalty,they are just mass hotel with no soul,no quality,no luxury.
The only good side when i returned a day later on 27th nov the guy who checked me in went extra mile to try to find my parcel and i can say he did a great job and he found it,i cant recal

We booked two superior rooms for a 5 night stay over the New Year period, to celebrate my wife's 50th birthday, travelling with our adult daughters. On booking I told the hotel that, having stayed three years previously and been impressed by our room on the 51st floor with a view of Times Square, I hoped they could provide equally impressive rooms for this special visit. When we arrived we were given adjoining rooms on the 37th floor with partial views of Times Square. When we reached the room we were appalled by the general tattiness, and more particularly, by the filthy state of it. The carpet was covered in bits, crumbs, fluff and a discarded nylon clothing label tie. I opened a bedside draw to store clothes and found screwed up, used tissues and empty pill foils. In the en-suite there was dust and dirt hanging from the extract grille and thick dust on the top of a picture frame. The carpet in and around the room door was almost black with dirt. Our daughters' room was equally tatty and dirty. We called the front desk to tell them that we were totally dissatisfied with the rooms and explained why. We were told that they did not have two adjacent rooms available other than these, but would send housekeeping to carry out more cleaning. I decided to take a shower and found there was no complimentary shower gell, so I called housekeeping. They said they only provide it on request, and would send some up, which they did. We went out for the evening and returned to find that the carpets had been vacuumed and the drawer cleared, but nothing else had been done.
The next morning I went to the desk to tell them that we were very unhappy, particularly as this was a special occasion. I told them again about the lack of cleanliness and general tattiness, and asked to be moved to a better room. I was told that this was not possible and that they would report my complaints to housekeeping. When we returned later in the day nothing had been done.
I went to the front desk again t

This hotel was the crown jewel of the Marriott corporation. I at this point, I would say it is an embarrassment that it still holds the Marriott name. The elevators are in disrepair, in fact my business partner was late to a meeting because he was trapped for almost 20 min in the elevator. The hotel looks like the last update was in 1990, we are now in 2016. The check in process was a disaster and the staff looked so unhappy to be at work.

If you plan on getting a rollaway, don't they will not give you one. I ended up sleeping on the couch bed which was a disaster.

I took 3 rooms in this hotel, including a suite for 3 nights and after all of that they screwed me out of all the Marriott Points and didn't count those nights towards the nights I stayed in a Marriott property. I would think spending $3000 on a hotel in a city where I could have stayed in a beautiful Hyatt across the street, would allow me the points and room nights. However, if you book this property on with a Marriott link provided to you from a trade show or event, you will not get points or nights.

I am done with Marriott

I stayed twice at the Paramount hotel in November and BOTH times I have had huge problems with the payments. They have charged me more than double the amount I booked my room for without even telling me. Today, one week after coming back, they have charged me 400€ extra after I checked with the hotel that my bills were closed. I have counted 7 different charges in my bank account under the name of PARAMOUNT HOTEL. I am a very experienced traveler and have stayed in all sorts of hotels around the world and this has NEVER happened. So if you don't want any horrible surprises in your bank accounts, it's best that you stay at another hotel.

My wife and I visit New York every December, last year we booked the Sanctuary for the first time and raved about it, we immediately booked up for this year and told friends that if they were heading to NY look no further than this hotel.
I got slightly worried when friends took our advice in the Summer and I received a text from them saying 'you are easily pleased !!!'.
On explanation our friends detailed they found the hotel OK but nothing to write home about so we were a little more apprehensive than we wanted to be.

We stayed 5 nights last week, our check in was as good as I expected when at 2.45pm a very helpful receptionist explained that she wanted to give us the best room available so needed 10 mins to prepare one, so we disappeared for a coffee and came back.
She made us feel very welcome and went to great lengths to explain she wanted our room to be perfect so needed a little time to ensure she could achieve this, when we returned we were given room 209.
During our coffee my wife and I were a little excited about the room we would be given given that our last room was excellent.

New York hotel rooms are generally small but this one was nowhere near the standard we had last year - it turns out that we were upgraded last year without being told (reception later confirmed this)which explained why we were so impressed on our previous visit, this time we had a standard room and whilst I know hotels in December aren't cheap if I'm paying almost $2000 for a 5 night stay I expect better than what we got.
At a guess I would say the room measures 150 square feet, and that includes bathroom area - this room also has no window, actually there is a small frosted window that you can't shift and it lets in virtually no natural light.
The room is also next door to a housekeeping cupboard and whilst we heard the housekeeping team I have to say we were never disturbed during sleeping hours.

I have stayed in a lot of hotels and I use to manage one for over 5 years so when I say this is the worst hotel that I have ever stayed in, I mean it. I booked this hotel a while back through a package deal through Expedia and thought that I was getting a great deal and this would be a nice a hotel.....WRONG!! The day before I left to start my vacation I went on the website to look up the address again and the information that I would need and happen to come across a few reviews by accident and they were all bad and I was hoping that maybe the review was written in anger because of the hidden resort fees (which by the way Expedia mentioned to me when I booked the hotel so I was aware that I would owe about 170 more when I got to the hotel), but the reviews turned out to be true. I waited to write this review the night before I leave because I really wanted it to be fair and to be able to give all of the correct information. So I do want to start by telling you that the location of this hotel couldn’t be better! It is right on Times Square and just down the street from where the ball drops on New Years Eve so you are in the heart of New York and near just about anything you want to do, another thing that I would like to correct is that in one review a customer said that the hot water didn’t get hot and they had to wait a long time but that wasn’t the case in my room, we had hot water all the time so maybe it was just in that room that they stayed in or maybe it depends on what room you are staying in. This is the only thing that I can say positive about this hotel. So lets start with all the things that are wrong with this hotel. First the hidden resort fee that a lot of people are unaware of until they check in is laughable. They gave me a list when I checked in and said that these are the facilities that they said that they are charging the $40 for: Wi-Fi, a phone, business center and fitness room. Really? Got checked into 327. Once in the room I immediately heard a loud pounding. I thought it was the room next door so I called downstairs to complain. From noon to 6pm straight I've heard and complained about the pounding. After 10pm... still pounding. I called to complain again and was informed that the fitness center was right above my room and its probably someone on the treadmill. I begged to me moved to another room but they said they had none available. The attendant said they'll send security to the gym the pounding continues. Its now well after 11pm and still no resolve. The worst hotel stay of my life!! Very bad idea to have a room right under the gym!!

I have an business associate that always stays here so I thought I would give it a try. Consistent long lines at the front desk. It my room phone did work, Internet did not work, refrigerator did not work and no way to adjust the thermostat (for me the room was cold). In the morning when I left for meeting I walk to a man at front desk walked him through the issues and left my room number and issues written in a note. When I got back that night later nothing was fixed except Internet, no message on other items. I called for a blanket so I was not cold. Will not stay again.

We stayed at The Surrey this past week for two nights thru jet setter. The rate was low so we thought we'd try it one more time. Now as the Taylor Swift lyrics go.."never ever again". Title 15 U.S. Code 1602 - Definitions and rules of construction j ) The terms open end credit plan and open end consumer credit plan mean a plan under which the creditor reasonably contemplates repeated transactions, which prescribes the terms of such transactions, and which provides for a finance charge which may be computed from time to time on the outstanding unpaid balance. A credit plan or open end consumer credit plan which is an open end credit plan or open end consumer credit plan within the meaning of the preceding sentence is an open end credit plan or open end consumer credit plan even if credit information is verified from time to time. 


Title 15 U.S. Code 1666d. Treatment of credit balances Whenever a credit balance in excess of {$1.00} is created in connection with a consumer credit transaction through ( 1 ) transmittal of funds to a creditor in excess of the total balance due on an account, ( 2 ) rebates of unearned finance charges or insurance premiums, or ( 3 ) amounts otherwise owed to or held for the benefit of an obligor, the creditor shall ( A ) credit the amount of the credit balance to the consumers account ; ( B ) refund any part of the amount of the remaining credit balance, upon request of the consumer ; and ( C ) make a good faith effort to refund to the consumer by cash, check, or money order any part of the amount of the credit balance remaining in the account for more than six months, except that no further action is required in any case in which the consumers current location is not known by the creditor and can not be traced through the consumers last known address or telephone number.
